title: Tele-Rehabilitation Specialist
description: A Tele-Rehabilitation Specialist delivers physiotherapy, occupational therapy, speech-language therapy, and psychological rehabilitation services remotely using video consultation platforms, wearable monitoring devices, and digital exercise apps. India's rural-urban healthcare gap, large population, and improved smartphone/internet penetration have made tele-rehabilitation critical for post-surgical recovery, stroke rehabilitation, orthopaedic recovery, and mental health support at scale. The NIMHANS tele-mental health initiative, PM e-Vidya, and the National Tele-Mental Health Programme (launched 2022) have catalysed this sector. Startups like PhysioTrack, Kinesics Health, and international platforms like Hinge Health and Sword Health are expanding in India.
personality_traits: You are empathetic and patient in supporting patient recovery
You are comfortable delivering care remotely via technology
You enjoy working with people across different stages of recovery
You are good at motivating and guiding patients through exercises
You are tech-savvy and comfortable with digital health platforms
You are organised in managing remote patient schedules and progress tracking
required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream (PCB); BPT (Bachelor of Physiotherapy), B.Sc. Occupational Therapy, B.Sc. Speech & Language Pathology, or B.Sc. Psychology
entrance_exams: NEET-UG (for BPT, B.Sc. Occupational Therapy at medical university-affiliated colleges)
State health sciences CETs (KCET, MHT-CET, AP EAMCET for health sciences)
No separate tele-rehabilitation exam; BPT/OT degree + telehealth certification
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCB → BPT (Bachelor of Physiotherapy, 4.5 years with internship) or B.Sc. Occupational Therapy/Speech-Language Pathology (4 years) → Register with relevant council (IAP — Indian Association of Physiotherapists) → Pursue telehealth certification: telerehabilitation competency programs, ABDM digital health certification → Learn tele-rehab platforms and wearable data interpretation → Join telehealth startups, hospitals with virtual care units, or start independent tele-rehab practice. Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
course_fee_inr: BPT (Govt medical college): INR 20,000‑60,000/yr; BPT (Private): INR 60,000‒2,50,000/yr; B.Sc. OT/SLP: INR 40,000‐1,50,000/yr; Telehealth/Tele-rehab certification: INR 10,000‑40,000
career_growth_path: Tele-Rehab Therapist → Senior Therapist → Clinical Lead → Head of Tele-Rehabilitation → Director of Digital Health Services

work_environment.places_of_work: Telehealth platforms (1mg, Practo, Mfine, Apollo Telehealth, DocsApp), hospitals with virtual care units (Fortis, Apollo, Manipal), tele-rehab startups (PhysioTrack, Kinesics Health), NIMHANS National Tele-Mental Health programme, home-based independent practice
work_environment.work_environment: Remote work from a home office or telehealth studio. Structured session scheduling with patients across time zones. Uses video consultation platforms, mobile health apps, and wearable data. Flexible working hours with both full-time and freelance models.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can launch an independent tele-rehab practice or a digital physiotherapy platform.

The programme, coordinated by NIMHANS, employs hundreds of remote rehabilitation and counselling specialists who deliver services to patients in tier-2 and tier-3 cities that previously had no access to specialist care.

title: Agrotech Entrepreneur / Farm Technology Specialist
description: Please note: A variant of 'Agritech Entrepreneur' already exists in the dataset. This entry specifically covers the Farm Technology Specialist role — a professional who implements, operates, and trains farmers on precision agriculture technologies: GPS-guided tractors, soil sensors, variable rate application systems, yield monitoring, farm management software (FMS), and IoT-based microclimate monitoring. India's agricultural technology sector is valued at USD 7.8 billion (2023) and is projected to reach USD 24 billion by 2030. Government programs: Digital Agriculture Mission, PM-KISAN digital integration, and the National Mission on Natural Farming all require farm technology implementation specialists.
personality_traits: You are passionate about both agriculture and technology
You enjoy hands-on fieldwork in farming environments
You are good at explaining technology to farmers in simple terms
You enjoy problem-solving in challenging rural environments
You are data-driven in measuring farm productivity improvements
You care about rural livelihoods and food security
required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream (PCM or PCB); B.Sc. Agriculture, B.Tech Agricultural Engineering, or B.Tech ECE/IT with agriculture interest
entrance_exams: ICAR AIEEA for B.Sc. Agriculture at central agricultural universities
JEE Main / State CETs for B.Tech Agricultural Engineering
State agricultural university entrance exams (UPCATET, AGRICET etc.)
GATE (AG) for M.Tech and PSU agricultural officer roles
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCM/PCB → B.Sc. Agriculture or B.Tech Agricultural Engineering (4 years) → Learn precision agriculture tools: GPS/GIS mapping, IoT sensors, drone technology, NDVI analysis → Learn FMS (Farm Management Software): Cropin, AgriStack, TeleSensors → Pursue short-term courses: Precision Agriculture training at ICAR/SAU, CropIn Academy → Join agritech startups, multinational agri-input companies, or government Digital Agriculture Mission. Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
course_fee_inr: B.Sc. Agriculture ICAR/SAU: INR 20,000‐1,50,000/yr; B.Tech Agricultural Engineering: INR 50,000‐2,00,000/yr; Precision Agriculture certificate (ICAR): INR 10,000‑50,000; CropIn Academy courses: INR 5,000‑30,000
career_growth_path: Field Agronomy Executive → Farm Technology Specialist → Precision Agriculture Manager → Regional Head → Head of Agritech Operations

work_environment.places_of_work: Agritech startups (CropIn, DeHaat, Ninjacart, Fasal, Intello Labs, AgroStar), multinational agri-input companies (Syngenta, Bayer Crop Science, BASF India), state government agricultural technology cells, cooperative societies, ICAR extension programs
work_environment.work_environment: Predominantly field-based across rural India. Travel to farming communities is core to the role. Mix of hands-on demonstration, training workshops, and data collection. Agritech startup roles may involve urban headquarters work alongside field rotations.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start an agritech consulting service, a precision farming input dealership, or a farm analytics startup.

title: Podcast Producer / Audio Content Creator
description: This is an updated and expanded entry for Podcast Producer / Audio Content Creator, focusing on the professional production side: A Podcast Producer plans, records, edits, and distributes audio content for individuals, brands, and media companies across platforms including Spotify, Apple Podcasts, JioSaavn, Google Podcasts, and Amazon Music. India's podcast market has grown 40%+ year-on-year with over 700 million podcast listeners projected by 2026, making it the world's third-largest podcast market. Companies like Spotify India, JioSaavn, IVM Podcasts, Kuku FM, and Hubhopper are building professional audio content teams. This career offers both employment and freelance/entrepreneurship paths. Note: A simpler 'Podcast Producer' entry exists in prior volumes; this entry covers the production-focused professional role with full industry detail.
personality_traits: You are creative and have a strong ear for audio quality
You are curious and love learning about diverse topics
You are organised in managing multi-episode production pipelines
You enjoy storytelling and content strategy
You are tech-savvy with audio editing and publishing tools
You are a strong communicator and enjoy working with hosts and guests
required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; BA/B.Sc. Mass Communication, Journalism, or a degree in any field combined with audio production skills
entrance_exams: IIMC (Indian Institute of Mass Communication) entrance exam for PG Diploma
Jamia Millia Islamia Mass Communication entrance exam
No specific national exam for podcast production; portfolio and certification-based entry
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 in any stream → BA/B.Sc. Mass Communication / Journalism / Creative Writing (3 years) → Learn audio production: Audacity, Adobe Audition, GarageBand, Reaper → Master RSS feed publishing and podcast SEO → Build a production portfolio with independently produced episodes → Pursue short courses: IIMC audio production, Coursera/Udemy audio and podcast courses → Join as Podcast Producer at a media company, production studio, or launch an independent production house. Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
course_fee_inr: BA Mass Communication (Govt Delhi University/Jadavpur): INR 15,000‑50,000/yr; IIMC PG Diploma: INR 60,000‐1,20,000/yr; Adobe Audition subscription: approx INR 1,700/month; Udemy audio production courses: INR 500–4,000 (on sale); Coursera Podcast Production specialisation: INR 2,000‐8,000/month
career_growth_path: Junior Podcast Producer → Podcast Producer → Senior Producer → Head of Audio / Editorial Director → VP of Content / Podcast Network Founder

work_environment.places_of_work: Spotify India (Mumbai/Bengaluru), JioSaavn, Kuku FM, IVM Podcasts, Hubhopper, Red FM, Times Radio, brands' content marketing teams, PR agencies, independent production studios; mostly hybrid/remote
work_environment.work_environment: Studio or home studio for recording and editing. Collaborative work with hosts, interviewers, and sales teams. Creative and intellectually stimulating. Freelance work is very common and growing. India's creator economy supports solo audio entrepreneurs.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can launch an independent podcast production studio, a branded podcast agency, or a podcast network.

title: Renewable Energy Project Developer
description: A Renewable Energy Project Developer identifies, structures, finances, permits, constructs, and commissions utility-scale renewable energy projects — solar parks, wind farms, green hydrogen facilities, and battery energy storage systems (BESS). India's renewable energy sector is the world's third largest with a 500 GW non-fossil capacity target by 2030 (PM Surya Ghar, KUSUM scheme, National Green Hydrogen Mission). MNRE, SECI (Solar Energy Corporation of India), NTPC Renewable Energy, and private developers (Adani Green, ReNew Power, Greenko, Tata Power Renewables, Hero Future Energies) offer strong careers. Global opportunities exist at Ørsted, Siemens Gamesa, Vestas, and ACCIONA for Indian engineering graduates.
personality_traits: You are passionate about clean energy and sustainability
You enjoy managing complex, multi-disciplinary infrastructure projects
You are comfortable with financial modelling and energy economics
You are a strong project manager who can work across technical and regulatory domains
You are good at stakeholder management including governments, investors, and communities
You are a strategic thinker who can navigate long project timelines
required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCM; B.Tech Electrical, Mechanical, Civil, or Chemical Engineering are the primary pathways
entrance_exams: JEE Main / JEE Advanced (for B.Tech at IITs/NITs)
GATE (EE, ME, CE) for M.Tech and PSU energy sector
MNRE/SECI recruitment exams for government energy development roles
CAT (for MBA energy management — senior developer pathway)
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCM → B.Tech Electrical / Mechanical / Civil Engineering (4 years) → Work in electrical design, grid engineering, or EPC (Engineering, Procurement, Construction) for 2-3 years → Gain project finance knowledge through MBA Energy or M.Tech Energy Management → Pursue certifications: NABCEP (for solar), IRENA renewable energy certificates, CFA (for project finance) → Join renewable energy project development companies or start a green energy IPP (Independent Power Producer). Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
course_fee_inr: B.Tech IIT/NIT EE/ME: INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; M.Tech Energy Technology IIT: INR 30,000‑80,000/yr; MBA Energy UPES/TERI: INR 3,00,000‐6,00,000 total; NABCEP Solar PV Installation certification: approx USD 500 (INR 42,000); MNRE/NISE solar training programs: INR 5,000‑20,000
career_growth_path: Project Engineer → Project Developer → Senior Project Developer → Project Development Manager → Head of Business Development → VP / Director of Projects → CEO of Renewable Energy Company

work_environment.places_of_work: Adani Green Energy, ReNew Power, Greenko, Tata Power Renewables, Hero Future Energies, NTPC Renewable Energy, Torrent Power, SECI, MNRE; EPC companies (Larsen & Toubro, Gamesa, Suzlon); international firms: Ørsted, Vestas, Siemens Gamesa India
work_environment.work_environment: Mix of office-based project development (financial modelling, permitting, procurement) and field visits to project sites in remote locations. Travel across India to wind corridors (Rajasthan, Gujarat, Tamil Nadu) and solar zones is common. Dynamic, high-stakes environment.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can develop independent power projects as an IPP (Independent Power Producer) or start a renewable energy EPC company.

title: Instructional Designer / Learning Experience Designer (LXD)
description: An Instructional Designer / Learning Experience Designer (LXD) designs and develops online and blended learning programs, e-learning modules, corporate training materials, and educational courses using pedagogical science and digital media tools. India's EdTech sector, valued at USD 7.5 billion in 2023 and projected to reach USD 30 billion by 2030, has created strong demand for instructional designers at Byju's, Unacademy, Coursera India, UpGrad, Simplilearn, LEAD School, and in corporate L&D (Learning & Development) departments of every large company. This career uniquely combines teaching expertise, psychology, and technology — offering high salaries for professionals who can design engaging learning experiences.
personality_traits: You have a strong passion for education and learning
You are creative in designing visually engaging content
You understand how people learn and retain information
You are organised and systematic in course development
You are tech-savvy with e-learning and graphic tools
You are a strong writer who can simplify complex concepts
required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; BA/B.Sc. Education, B.Ed, BA Psychology, or any degree with strong communication and technology skills
entrance_exams: B.Ed entrance exams (state-level and central: IGNOU B.Ed, DU B.Ed)
MA Education / M.Ed entrance exams at central universities
No specific national exam for instructional design; portfolio-based hiring
CTET (Central Teacher Eligibility Test) for school-level educational design roles in government
CAT/MBA exams for L&D management roles at corporate level
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 in any stream → BA/B.Sc. in any discipline (3 years) → B.Ed (1-2 years) or M.Ed / MA Education / M.Sc. Educational Technology → Learn Instructional Design models: ADDIE, SAM, Bloom's Taxonomy → Master e-learning authoring tools: Articulate Storyline 360, Adobe Captivate, iSpring Suite → Learn LMS (Learning Management Systems): Moodle, Canvas, Blackboard → Build a portfolio of designed courses → Join EdTech companies, corporate L&D departments, or universities. Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
course_fee_inr: BA (Govt university): INR 10,000‑40,000/yr; B.Ed (Govt): INR 15,000‑60,000/yr; M.Ed (Govt): INR 15,000‑60,000/yr; Articulate 360 subscription: approx USD 1,299/yr (INR 1,08,000/yr); Coursera Instructional Design certificate: INR 2,000‐8,000/month
career_growth_path: Junior Instructional Designer → Instructional Designer → Senior Instructional Designer → Learning Experience Design Lead → Head of L&D / Director of Learning Design → Chief Learning Officer (CLO)

work_environment.places_of_work: EdTech companies (Byju's, Unacademy, UpGrad, Simplilearn, Coursera India, LEAD School), corporate L&D departments (all large Indian and multinational companies), universities and higher education institutions, NGOs with education focus (ASER, Pratham)
work_environment.work_environment: Office or hybrid/fully remote. Creative, project-based work with content subject matter experts and graphic designers. Uses cloud-based collaboration tools (Google Docs, Notion, Figma) and e-learning authoring software. Generally moderate-paced with clear project milestones.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a freelance instructional design studio, a corporate training consultancy, or an online course creation agency.

title: Cyber Threat Intelligence Analyst
description: A Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) Analyst collects, analyses, and communicates information about current and emerging cyber threats — ransomware groups, nation-state attackers, phishing campaigns, and zero-day exploits — to help organisations proactively defend their digital infrastructure. As India faces 3.3 million unfilled cybersecurity positions (NASSCOM 2024) and a 40% year-on-year rise in reported cybercrime (CERT-In), CTI is one of the most urgent and well-compensated cybersecurity specialisations. The financial sector (RBI-mandated cyber frameworks), BFSI, defence, and IT services companies all hire CTI analysts. Indian students can enter via B.Tech CSE + cybersecurity certifications or specialised forensics/security programs at CDAC and DSCI.
personality_traits: You are deeply curious about how cyber attacks work
You enjoy research and open-source intelligence (OSINT) analysis
You are systematic and thorough in threat documentation
You are comfortable working with adversarial mindsets
You are a strong communicator who can write clear threat briefs
You thrive in high-pressure, fast-moving security environments
required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCM; B.Tech CSE/IT/ECE or B.Sc. Computer Science strongly preferred
entrance_exams: JEE Main / State CETs for B.Tech CSE/IT programs
CDAC C-CAT for PG Diploma in Cyber Security (C-DAC)
NIELIT/CDAC cybersecurity exam for government cyber security roles
Company-specific security hiring assessments (CHFI, CEH prerequisite tests)
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCM → B.Tech CSE/IT/ECE (4 years) → Learn networking fundamentals, operating systems (Linux, Windows), and basic malware analysis → Pursue CDAC PG Diploma in Cyber Security / DSCI CIPP training → Earn certifications: CEH (EC-Council Certified Ethical Hacker), GIAC GCTI (Certified Threat Intelligence Analyst), MITRE ATT&CK training → Build CTI skills: threat modelling (STIX/TAXII), dark web monitoring, malware reverse engineering (basic), OSINT tools → Join cyber security operations centres (SOC), CERT-In, or financial sector cyber teams. Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
course_fee_inr: B.Tech IIT/NIT CSE: INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; CDAC PG Diploma Cyber Security: INR 70,000‐1,50,000; CEH certification (EC-Council): approx INR 25,000‑50,000; GIAC GCTI: approx USD 999 (INR 83,000); MITRE ATT&CK online training: free
career_growth_path: Junior Security Analyst → Cyber Threat Intelligence Analyst → Senior CTI Analyst → CTI Team Lead → Head of Cyber Threat Intelligence → CISO (Chief Information Security Officer)

work_environment.places_of_work: CERT-In (Government of India), National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC), DSCI (Data Security Council of India), banks and financial institutions (HDFC, ICICI, SBI Cyber Cell), IT services (TCS Cyber Security, Infosys Cyber Defense), global security firms (Palo Alto, CrowdStrike India, Mandiant India)
work_environment.work_environment: Office or remote SOC (Security Operations Centre) environment. Shift-based monitoring for threat events. High-pressure during active incidents. Uses threat intelligence platforms (TIPs), SIEM tools, and dark web monitoring dashboards. Strong team collaboration culture.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a cyber threat intelligence advisory firm or a security consulting practice.

scholarships: MeitY Cyber Suraksha awareness and skill development grants
EC-Council Foundation Scholarships for CEH/CHFI certifications
NASSCOM FutureSkills Prime cybersecurity scholarship programs
loans: SBI Scholar Loan for B.Tech/M.Tech students
HDFC Credila, Avanse, InCred Education Loans

government: C-DAC (Centre for Development of Advanced Computing) — PG Diploma Cyber Security (multiple centres: Noida, Hyderabad, Bengaluru, Chennai)
NIELIT New Delhi — Cyber Security certification courses
private: DSCI (Data Security Council of India) — CIPP/CISM preparatory courses
EC-Council iLearn (online): CEH, CHFI, CTIA certifications
ISOEH (Indian School of Ethical Hacking), Kolkata
Quick Heal Academy, Pune — Cyber Threat Intelligence training
Manipal University — B.Tech / M.Tech Cyber Security
distance_learning: Coursera: IBM Cybersecurity Analyst Professional Certificate
SANS Institute online: GIAC GCTI self-study
Cybrary.it: Threat Intelligence Analyst career path (online)
MeitY NIC eLearning Portal: Cyber Security courses (free for govt employees)

example_from_field: India's CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team), under MeitY and led by Dr. Sanjay Bahl (DG), coordinates national-level cyber threat intelligence across all critical sectors. Its Threat Hunting and CTI teams have detected and neutralised multiple nation-state cyber operations targeting Indian critical infrastructure including the power grid and banking system, creating strong career demand for trained CTI analysts.
source: CERT-In Annual Report 2023-24; NASSCOM India Cybersecurity Report 2024; DSCI India Cyber Security Market Report 2024; GrowYouth India Cybersecurity Career Report 2025; EC-Council GCTI certification page 2025
availability: India (critical national shortage of cybersecurity professionals) and Global; Indian CTI analysts in high demand in USA, UK, Singapore, UAE

title: Textile and Fashion Technology Specialist
description: A Textile and Fashion Technology Specialist applies scientific and engineering knowledge to develop new fibres, fabric structures, dyes, finishes, and manufacturing processes for the textile and apparel industry. India is the world's second-largest producer and exporter of textiles and apparel (IBEF 2024), with the sector contributing USD 44 billion in exports annually and targeting USD 100 billion by 2030 under the PM MITRA (Mega Integrated Textile Region and Apparel) scheme. The sector employs over 45 million people and needs specialists for technical textiles, smart fabrics, sustainable dyeing, and digital textile printing. Indian graduates from NIFT, ICT, and NIFTs are highly sought after by domestic and international fashion brands.
personality_traits: You are creative and have a keen eye for materials and design
You are scientifically curious about fibres, polymers, and chemistry
You enjoy hands-on work in labs and production floors
You are detail-oriented in quality and process optimisation
You are interested in sustainable and circular fashion
You enjoy combining science, design, and technology
required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream (PCM or PCB) for B.Tech Textile Engineering; any stream for B.Sc. Textile Design/Technology; B.Des Fashion Design requires aptitude tests
entrance_exams: NIFT Entrance Exam (for B.Des / B.F.Tech at National Institute of Fashion Technology)
JEE Main / State CETs (for B.Tech Textile Engineering at NIT Jalandhar, HBTU Kanpur)
ICT Mumbai (Institute of Chemical Technology) entrance exam for Fibres & Textile processing
State textile technology college entrance exams
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 in Science or any stream → B.Tech Textile Engineering / B.F.Tech (Fashion Technology) / B.Sc. Textile Technology (4 years) → Learn fibre science, fabric engineering, wet processing (dyeing, printing, finishing), CAD/CAM for textiles → Specialise in technical textiles, sustainable fashion, or digital printing → M.Tech Textile Engineering or M.Des for advanced roles → Join textile mills, apparel manufacturers, technical textile firms, or NIFT alumni network in fashion industry. Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
course_fee_inr: B.F.Tech NIFT (Govt): INR 1,50,000‐2,50,000/yr; B.Tech Textile Engineering NIT Jalandhar: INR 80,000‐1,50,000/yr; B.Sc. Textile (ICT Mumbai): INR 20,000‑60,000/yr; Private B.F.Tech: INR 1,50,000‒3,50,000/yr
career_growth_path: Trainee Technologist → Textile/Fashion Technologist → Senior Technologist → R&D Manager → Head of Textile Technology / Head of Product Development → Director R&D

work_environment.places_of_work: Textile mills (Raymond, Welspun, Arvind Mills, Vardhman Textiles), garment exporters, technical textile companies (Garware Technical Fibres, SRF, Mafatlal), NIFT research centres, fashion brands (Fabindia, Manyavar, H&M India sourcing offices), DRDO technical textile projects
work_environment.work_environment: Mix of laboratory (fibre and fabric testing), production floor (mill or manufacturing), and design office. Travel to mills, factories, and trade fairs is common. Strong team collaboration between chemists, engineers, designers, and production managers.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a technical textile startup, a sustainable fashion brand, or a textile testing and certification laboratory.

scholarships: Ministry of Textiles merit scholarship for NIFT students
PM MITRA scheme skill development scholarships
ATIRA (Ahmedabad Textile Industry Research Association) fellowships
State government scholarships for textile engineering students (UP, Gujarat, TN)
loans: Education loans from nationalised banks for B.Tech/B.F.Tech programs
SBI Student Loan for NIFT and NIT students

example_from_field: Nisha Chitale, Director of R&D at Welspun India (a USD 2.5 billion global home textile giant), leads innovations in sustainable smart textiles, including Welspun's patented HygroCotton technology that is exported to Walmart, Target, and IKEA globally. India's Garware Technical Fibres (Pune) exports high-performance nets and fibres to the world's fishing, sports, and military sectors — all developed by Indian textile technology graduates.
source: Ministry of Textiles India Annual Report 2023-24; IBEF Indian Textile Sector Report 2024; PM MITRA Scheme Guidelines 2023; NIFT Annual Report 2023-24; Welspun India Annual Report 2024
availability: India (world's 2nd largest textile exporter; strongly growing sector) and Global; Indian textile technologists hired by H&M, Zara, Marks & Spencer, IKEA sourcing teams

title: Robotics Software Engineer
description: A Robotics Software Engineer develops the software systems that enable robots to perceive their environment, plan actions, and execute tasks autonomously — including robot operating system (ROS) integration, computer vision, motion planning, sensor fusion, and human-robot interaction (HRI). India's robotics market is growing at 14% CAGR and is projected to reach USD 4.3 billion by 2027. Manufacturing automation (automotive, electronics, pharma), warehouse robotics (Amazon, Flipkart, Delhivery), agricultural robots, and surgical robots are key growth areas. Global robotics companies (Boston Dynamics, iRobot, ABB, KUKA, Fanuc) and Indian startups (GreyOrange, Addverb Technologies, Asimov Robotics) offer careers for robotics software engineers. IIT graduates are globally competitive in this field.
personality_traits: You are passionate about robots, automation, and AI systems
You have strong programming skills in Python and C++
You enjoy solving perception and motion planning challenges
You are comfortable with both hardware and software integration
You are systematic in debugging complex autonomous systems
You enjoy hands-on building and testing of robotic prototypes
required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCM; B.Tech/BE in CSE, ECE, Mechanical Engineering, or Mechatronics
entrance_exams: JEE Main / JEE Advanced (for B.Tech at IITs/NITs)
State engineering CETs for B.Tech programs
GATE (CS/EC/ME) for M.Tech robotics and PSU automation roles
Company-specific tests for robotics software engineer roles (GreyOrange, Addverb)
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCM → B.Tech CSE / ECE / Mechanical / Mechatronics (4 years) → Learn ROS (Robot Operating System), Python, C++, and OpenCV → Learn motion planning: MoveIt!, OMPL → Gain experience with simulation tools: Gazebo, RViz, Webots, CoppeliaSim → M.Tech in Robotics/Mechatronics (IIT Kanpur, IIT Bombay, IISc, IIIT Hyderabad) → Build projects: autonomous navigation, robotic arm control, drone autonomy → Join robotics companies via campus or off-campus hiring. Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
course_fee_inr: B.Tech IIT/NIT: INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; M.Tech Robotics IIT/IISc: INR 30,000‑80,000/yr; ROS courses (The Construct, Udemy): INR 5,000‑50,000; NVIDIA Jetson robotics training: INR 10,000‑30,000
career_growth_path: Robotics Software Engineer → Senior Robotics Engineer → Lead Robotics Engineer → Robotics System Architect → Head of Robotics R&D → CTO / Director of Engineering

work_environment.places_of_work: GreyOrange (Gurugram/Singapore), Addverb Technologies (Noida), Asimov Robotics (Kerala), Amazon Robotics India (Hyderabad), Tata Elxsi (Bengaluru), Wipro Robotics, ISRO robotics, DRDO robotic systems; international: Boston Dynamics, ABB, KUKA, Fanuc India
work_environment.work_environment: Lab and office environment with robotic test beds. Mix of software development, hardware integration, and field deployment. Highly collaborative with mechanical, electronics, and AI teams. Fast-paced at startups; more structured at large automation companies. Travel to client factories for deployment.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a robotics startup in industrial automation, agricultural robots, or medical robotics.

scholarships: AICTE Pragati Scholarship for girl students in technical education
DST-SERB grants for robotics research projects
DRDO research fellowships for robotics system development
Google Research India grants for robotics and AI research
IIT/NIT merit scholarships for M.Tech students
loans: SBI Scholar Loan for IIT/NIT/IISc students
HDFC Credila, Avanse, InCred Education Loans

GreyOrange was valued at USD 1 billion (unicorn status) in 2019 and employs 400+ robotics engineers across India, USA, and Singapore.
source: NASSCOM India Robotics Market Report 2024; GreyOrange investor materials 2024; Addverb Technologies Annual Report 2024; IIT Kanpur M.Tech Robotics program page 2025; Amazon Robotics India job postings 2025
availability: India (growing rapidly) and Global; Indian robotics engineers hired by Boston Dynamics, ABB, Fanuc, and Amazon Robotics

title: Game Developer / Video Game Programmer
description: A Game Developer / Video Game Programmer designs, codes, and debugs interactive video game systems — game engines, physics simulations, AI opponent behaviour, networking, rendering pipelines, and user interfaces — for mobile, PC, console, and AR/VR platforms. India's gaming and interactive entertainment market is the fastest growing in the world at 28% CAGR, valued at USD 3.8 billion in 2023 and projected to reach USD 9 billion by 2027 (FICCI-EY 2024). India has 568 million gamers (2024), the world's largest gaming user base. Companies like Nazara Technologies, Mobile Premier League (MPL), Dream11, WinZO, Nodwin Gaming, 99 Games, and international giants (EA, Ubisoft, Zynga India, Gameloft India) offer strong careers for game developers.
personality_traits: You are passionate about video games and interactive experiences
You have strong programming skills and logical thinking
You enjoy creative problem-solving in real-time systems
You have an eye for visual design and player experience
You enjoy continuous improvement and iterative development
You are a self-directed learner who builds game projects independently
required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCM recommended; B.Tech CSE/IT, B.Sc. Computer Science, or B.Sc. Animation & Game Design are primary entry paths
entrance_exams: JEE Main / State CETs for B.Tech CSE/IT programs
UCEED / NID entrance exam for design-oriented game design programs
Frameboxx, Arena Animation entrance tests for B.Sc. Animation & Game Design
Company-specific technical assessments (Unity, Unreal Engine proficiency tests)
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCM or any stream → B.Tech CSE / B.Sc. Computer Science / B.Sc. Animation & Game Design (3-4 years) → Learn game engines: Unity (C#) and Unreal Engine (C++/Blueprints) → Learn game mathematics: linear algebra, physics simulation, quaternions → Build a game portfolio: 3-5 completed game projects on itch.io or Play Store → Contribute to game jams (Global Game Jam, Ludum Dare) → Specialise: graphics programming, gameplay systems, multiplayer networking, AI, or mobile optimization → Apply to game studios or launch as indie developer. Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
course_fee_inr: B.Tech IIT/NIT CSE: INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; B.Sc. Animation & Game Design (Arena, Frameboxx): INR 80,000‒2,00,000/yr; Unity Learn Premium: approx USD 40/month (INR 3,300/month); Unreal Online Learning: free; Udemy game development courses: INR 500–4,000 (on sale)
career_growth_path: Junior Game Developer → Game Programmer → Senior Game Developer → Lead Programmer → Technical Director → Game Studio Head / CTO

work_environment.places_of_work: Nazara Technologies (Mumbai), Mobile Premier League/MPL (Bengaluru), Dream11 (Mumbai), WinZO (Delhi), Gameloft India (Hyderabad), Zynga India (Bengaluru), EA India (Hyderabad), Ubisoft Mumbai, Nodwin Gaming, 99 Games (Udupi), indie studios
work_environment.work_environment: Creative tech studio environment. Mix of team-based development sprints, playtesting sessions, and crunch periods near launch deadlines. Strong use of version control (Git), issue trackers (JIRA), and collaboration tools. Can be fully remote for indie developers. High job satisfaction for games enthusiasts.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can launch an indie game studio, build and self-publish mobile/PC games, or create game development tools and assets.

scholarships: Epic Games MegaGrants: funding for games, tools, and educational projects built with Unreal Engine
Unity Technologies charity grants for student game developers
NASSCOM FutureSkills Prime Game Development scholarships
Global Game Jam site fee waivers for student participants
loans: Education loans from nationalised banks for B.Tech/B.Sc. programs

Nazara's portfolio includes Nodwin Gaming, Sportskeeda, and Kiddopia — with combined revenues of over INR 1,000 crore. Indian game developers at MPL and WinZO have built platforms with 100 million+ users, proving India's scale in the global gaming industry.

title: Marine Engineer
description: A Marine Engineer designs, maintains, operates, and repairs the propulsion systems, electrical systems, and mechanical equipment of ships, submarines, offshore platforms, and marine vessels. India's shipping and maritime sector is strategically important: India has the world's largest fleet of merchant marine officers, handles 90% of its trade by sea, and is building the Sagarmala project (USD 82 billion port-led development). The Directorate General of Shipping (DGS) regulates the profession. Indian Marine Engineers work aboard merchant ships globally, earning substantial dollar-denominated salaries, as well as in shipyards (Cochin Shipyard, Mazagon Dock, L&T Shipbuilding), offshore oil rigs (ONGC), and navies.
personality_traits: You have a passion for ships, the sea, and mechanical systems
You are adventurous and comfortable with extended periods at sea
You are technically skilled and enjoy solving mechanical problems
You are disciplined and follow safety protocols strictly
You are adaptable and calm in challenging marine environments
You enjoy the combination of engineering work and global travel
required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCM (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics); B.Tech in Marine Engineering or B.E. Mechanical/Electrical with marine certification is required
entrance_exams: IMU CET (Indian Maritime University Common Entrance Test) for B.Tech Marine Engineering
DMET entrance exam for Marine Engineering colleges in West Bengal
JEE Main for B.Tech Mechanical/Naval Architecture at NIT Calicut, IIT Kharagpur
DGS (Directorate General of Shipping) written examination for marine officer certification (Class 4, Class 2, Class 1)
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCM → B.Tech Marine Engineering (4 years) from IMU, MERI, DMET or DGS-approved institute → Complete sea training as Engine Cadet (6-12 months) → Appear for DGS MEO Class 4 Certificate of Competency → Progress through Class 4 → Class 3 → Class 2 → Chief Engineer's Certificate (Class 1) → Earn foreign-going certificates (STCW) for international ship employment. Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
course_fee_inr: B.Tech Marine Engineering (IMU, Govt): INR 1,50,000‐2,50,000/yr; B.Tech Marine Engineering (Private, approved): INR 2,50,000‐4,00,000/yr; DGS COC exam fees: INR 5,000‑15,000 per level; STCW certification courses: INR 20,000‑50,000
career_growth_path: Engine Cadet → 4th Engineer → 3rd Engineer → 2nd Engineer → Chief Engineer (equivalent to CMD of ship's engine room) → Superintendent Engineer (shore-based) → Fleet Manager / Head of Marine Operations

work_environment.places_of_work: Merchant ships (tankers, container ships, bulk carriers, LNG carriers) of global shipping companies (MSC, Maersk, MOL, K-Line, Stolt-Nielsen) operating worldwide; Indian Navy; ONGC offshore platforms; Cochin Shipyard, Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders, L&T Shipbuilding; DRDO naval labs
work_environment.work_environment: Ship-based: rotational contracts (typically 3-4 months on / 3-4 months leave). Physical, hands-on engine room work. Demanding but with substantial time off between contracts. Shore-based senior roles are 9-to-5 office work. Dollar-denominated international salaries are highly attractive.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a ship management company, a marine consultancy, or a marine equipment dealership.

scholarships: Directorate General of Shipping merit awards for top marine engineering graduates
Shipping Corporation of India (SCI) cadetship sponsorship program
State maritime board scholarships for maritime students
loans: Education loans from nationalised banks for B.Tech Marine Engineering
Cadetship sponsorships from shipping companies (reduces loan requirement)

The late Captain Swaroop Krishen Kaul, the first Indian to command an international tanker fleet as Chief Engineer and later Fleet Superintendent, exemplifies the career trajectory: from sea cadet to managing a fleet of 15+ vessels from a shore-based operations centre.

title: Food Processing Technology Specialist
description: A Food Processing Technology Specialist applies food science, engineering, and safety knowledge to develop, optimise, and quality-assure food and beverage processing operations — including pasteurisation, extrusion, spray drying, retort processing, canning, and IQF (Individually Quick Freezing) for the packaged food, dairy, beverage, and nutraceuticals industries. India's food processing sector is the world's sixth largest with a market value of USD 330 billion (2024) and a government push under the PM Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PM FME) and PLI for food processing. Companies like ITC Foods, Nestle India, HUL, Britannia, Amul, Haldirams, Mother Dairy, and Patanjali all hire food processing specialists.
personality_traits: You are interested in food science and the chemistry of food
You are detail-oriented in quality and safety compliance
You enjoy process optimisation and industrial engineering
You are curious about nutrition, flavour science, and product formulation
You are systematic in documentation and regulatory compliance (FSSAI)
You enjoy working at the intersection of science and manufacturing
required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream (PCB or PCM); B.Tech Food Technology / B.Sc. Food Science / B.Tech Dairy Technology are primary entry paths
entrance_exams: ICAR AIEEA for B.Tech Food Technology at central agricultural universities
JEE Main / State CETs for B.Tech Food Technology at NITs
State agricultural university entrance exams (UPCATET, KCET etc.)
GATE (XL — Life Sciences / Food Technology) for M.Tech and PSU roles
FSSAI Recruitment exam for Central Food Safety Officer (Group B)
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCB/PCM → B.Tech Food Technology / B.Sc. Food Science & Technology (4 years) → M.Tech Food Engineering & Technology (for R&D/senior roles) → Learn HACCP, GMP, ISO 22000, FSSC 22000 food safety systems → Gain hands-on experience in processing labs → Obtain FSSAI food safety supervisor certification → Join food companies, dairy cooperatives, export units, or government food testing labs. Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
course_fee_inr: B.Tech Food Technology ICAR/SAU: INR 20,000‐1,50,000/yr; B.Tech Food Technology NIT/CFTRI-affiliated: INR 50,000‐2,00,000/yr; M.Tech Food Engineering (IIT/CFTRI): INR 30,000‑80,000/yr; HACCP certification: INR 10,000‑30,000; ISO 22000 Lead Auditor: INR 30,000‑70,000
career_growth_path: Food Technologist Trainee → Food Technologist → Senior Food Technologist → R&D Manager / Quality Manager → Head of Manufacturing / Head of R&D → Director of Food Technology

work_environment.places_of_work: ITC Foods, Nestle India, HUL Foods, Britannia, Parle Products, Amul (GCMMF), Mother Dairy, Haldirams, Marico, Patanjali, Dabur India, CFTRI (Mysuru), FSSAI, state food testing labs, export-oriented food processing units
work_environment.work_environment: Factory/processing plant environment with cleanroom-style production floors. Lab-based quality testing and product development. Involves HACCP documentation, batch record review, and regulatory audit preparation. Mix of factory shift work and R&D lab work at senior levels.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a food processing unit under PM FME scheme, a food testing laboratory, or a food product startup.

scholarships: ICAR Junior Research Fellowship for food science students
CFTRI (Central Food Technological Research Institute) merit fellowships
DST-SERB early career grants for food technology research
PM FME scheme grants for food micro-enterprises (for entrepreneurs)
loans: Education loans from nationalised banks for B.Tech/M.Tech Food Technology
MUDRA Loan for food processing micro-enterprises

availability: India (world's 6th largest food processing industry) and Global; Indian food technologists hired by global FMCG companies (Nestle, Unilever, P&G) in their international R&D centres

title: Behavioural Economist / Nudge Policy Analyst
description: A Behavioural Economist applies insights from psychology, cognitive science, and economics to understand how people make real-world decisions — often irrationally — and uses 'nudges' to design policies, products, and services that guide people towards better outcomes (savings, health, tax compliance, voting, energy use). India's government formally adopted behavioural economics through NITI Aayog's Behavioural Insights Unit (BIU) and the Tata Trusts Nudge Foundation. Globally, behavioural economics is integral to the UK's Behavioural Insights Team, Australia's BETA, and USAID's development economics work. Indian graduates with economics, psychology, and data science skills can access high-value roles in policy, fintech, and development organisations.
personality_traits: You are curious about why people make irrational decisions
You enjoy applying scientific research to real-world policy problems
You have strong analytical and quantitative research skills
You are a creative thinker who designs experiments and interventions
You are a clear communicator who can translate research for policymakers
You are passionate about social impact and human welfare
required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; BA Economics, B.Sc. Psychology, B.Sc. Mathematics/Statistics, or B.Tech (for data-intensive research roles) are all viable pathways
entrance_exams: CUET (Common University Entrance Test) for BA Economics at central universities
IIT JAM (for M.Sc. Economics at IITs)
DSE MA Economics entrance exam (Delhi School of Economics)
IGIDR PhD entrance exam (Indira Gandhi Institute of Development Research)
No direct national exam for behavioural economics; advanced degree in Economics + research experience is the path
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 in any stream → BA Economics / B.Sc. Psychology / B.Sc. Statistics (3 years) → MA/M.Sc. Economics (with Behavioural Economics specialisation) → Develop RCT (Randomised Controlled Trial) and field experiment skills → Learn data analysis: R, Python, Stata for econometric work → PhD strongly recommended for research leadership roles (stipend-funded) → Join NITI Aayog BIU, J-PAL South Asia, the Nudge Foundation, or fintech companies. Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
course_fee_inr: BA Economics (Govt Delhi University/JNU): INR 5,000‑20,000/yr; MA Economics DSE: INR 10,000‑30,000/yr; MA Economics (Private FLAME, Ashoka): INR 5,00,000‐8,00,000 total; PhD (IGIDR, ISI, IIM research programs): INR 0 (UGC/ICSSR fellowship funded); Coursera Behavioral Economics courses: INR 2,000‐8,000/month
career_growth_path: Research Analyst / Junior Economist → Behavioural Economist → Senior Research Fellow → Principal Researcher / Head of Insights → Director of Policy Research → Chief Economist

work_environment.places_of_work: NITI Aayog BIU, Nudge Foundation (Bengaluru), J-PAL South Asia (Chennai/Cambridge), IFMR LEAD, Tata Trusts, EY Economics Advisory, McKinsey Center for Societal Benefit through Healthcare, Reserve Bank of India research wing, fintechs (Paytm, CRED, Jupiter — behavioural product design)
work_environment.work_environment: Research and policy office environment. Mix of literature review, experiment design, field data collection, statistical analysis, and policy brief writing. International conference travel possible at senior levels. Intellectually stimulating with purpose-driven colleagues.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a behavioural insights consulting firm, a policy advisory, or a nudge-based app.

scholarships: UGC Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) for M.Phil/PhD in Economics
ICSSR (Indian Council of Social Science Research) fellowships
J-PAL South Asia Research Fellowship (for development economics research)
DSE (Delhi School of Economics) merit scholarships for MA Economics
Ashoka University Scholars Program (need-based full scholarship)
loans: Education loans from nationalised banks for MA/M.Sc. Economics
Note: PhD programs at IGIDR, ISI, IIM are fully stipend-funded — loans rarely needed
HDFC Credila, Avanse for private university programs

Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU), School of Social Sciences — MA Economics
IGIDR Mumbai (Indira Gandhi Institute of Development Research) — M.Sc./PhD Economics

IIT Bombay/Madras/Delhi — M.Sc. Economics / MA Economics
private: Ashoka University, Sonipat — BA and MA Economics (top liberal arts)
Krea University, Andhra Pradesh — BA/MA Economics
Nudge Foundation, Bengaluru — behavioural insights applied programs
J-PAL South Asia, Chennai — research associate programs

example_from_field: Saugato Datta, co-founder of ideas42 (a global behavioural design firm with India operations), has worked with NITI Aayog, the Gates Foundation, and the World Bank to use behavioural nudges to improve sanitation adoption, financial savings, and healthcare compliance in India. India's Nudge Foundation (Bengaluru) has delivered interventions reaching over 5 million people across 12 Indian states using purely behavioural approaches — without additional financial subsidies.
source: NITI Aayog Behavioural Insights Unit 2023; Nudge Foundation India Impact Report 2024; J-PAL South Asia Research Portfolio 2024; Ashoka University Economics faculty research 2024; J-PAL Poverty Action Lab global operations 2025
availability: India (growing through NITI Aayog and development sector) and Global; Indian behavioural economists recruited by World Bank, USAID, UN agencies, and global consulting firms

metadata.description: 50 new careers not present in Volumes 1-4, researched for Indian students with India-specific salary, institute, scholarship, and scope data. Sources: Glassdoor India (2025-2026), WEF Future of Jobs Report 2025, India Skills Report 2025, AICTE, UGC, SEBI, IBBI, NationalCareerService.nic.in, Naukri.com, AmbitionBox, Salary.com India.

careers.title: Quantum Computing Engineer
careers.description: A Quantum Computing Engineer designs, builds, and programs quantum processors and quantum algorithms that exploit superposition and entanglement to solve problems far beyond the reach of classical computers. In India, the National Quantum Mission (NQM) launched in 2023 with a budget of ₹6,003 crore is creating institutional demand at IISc, IITs, and TIFR. Indian students can access global opportunities at IBM Quantum, Google Quantum AI, and D-Wave while contributing to India's own quantum roadmap. The role requires deep knowledge of linear algebra, quantum mechanics, and programming with frameworks such as Qiskit and Cirq.
personality_traits: You are fascinated by physics and mathematics at a deep level
You enjoy solving problems that have no classical solution
You are comfortable with extreme ambiguity in a frontier science
You are persistent and curious — willing to spend months on a single problem
You enjoy collaboration between physicists, engineers, and computer scientists
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCM (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics); strong performance in Physics and Mathematics is essential.
entrance_exams: JEE Advanced (for IITs offering B.Tech in Engineering Physics or CSE)
GATE – Physics / Computer Science (for M.Tech and research fellowships)
CSIR-UGC NET – Physical Sciences (for research fellowships)
JEST (Joint Entrance Screening Test for PhD in Physics)
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCM → B.Tech in Engineering Physics, CSE or ECE (4 years) OR B.Sc. Physics (3 years) → M.Sc./M.Tech in Quantum Computing, Quantum Information, or Applied Physics (2 years) → PhD in Quantum Computing or Quantum Information is highly preferred for research roles. Gain proficiency in Qiskit (IBM), Cirq (Google), or PennyLane (Xanadu). Pursue IBM Quantum Developer Certification. Apply for PMRF (Prime Minister's Research Fellowship) or NQM fellowships for funded PhD research. Entry-level industry roles are also accessible with a strong M.Tech and internship at a quantum lab.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 50,000 – 2,50,000 per year (government IITs/NITs); INR 1,50,000 – 4,00,000 per year (private institutions); IBM Quantum Developer Certification: INR 10,000 – 25,000
careers.career_growth_path: Quantum Research Intern → Junior Quantum Engineer → Quantum Software Developer → Senior Quantum Engineer → Quantum Architect → Head of Quantum Research / Chief Quantum Officer

work_environment.places_of_work: IITs, IISc, TIFR, C-DAC, TCS Research, IBM India Research Labs, startups such as BosonQ Psi (Pune), QpiAI (Bengaluru), and global companies (IBM, Google, Microsoft, IonQ) accessible via remote roles.
work_environment.work_environment: Highly research-intensive environment involving code, simulation software, and occasional hardware lab access. Mostly hybrid or remote for software-side roles; lab-based for hardware research. Strong academic culture with publication expectations in research roles.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can found a quantum software startup or a quantum-as-a-service (QaaS) platform — a booming segment globally.

scholarships: Prime Minister's Research Fellowship (PMRF) – up to ₹80,000/month for PhD at IITs/IISc
National Quantum Mission (NQM) Research Fellowships – DST, Govt. of India
CSIR-UGC Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) for Physical Sciences
INSPIRE Fellowship (DST) – for PhD in basic sciences including Physics
National Scholarship Portal: www.scholarships.gov.in (UGC/AICTE & State schemes)
loans: VidyaLakshmi portal: www.vidyalakshmi.co.in (Department of Financial Services, MoF)
Education loans from all nationalised banks (SBI, PNB, Bank of Baroda — up to ₹20 lakh without collateral)
SBI Scholar Loan Scheme for IIT/IISc admissions
HDFC Credila, Avanse, and InCred for M.Tech / postgraduate abroad
State student credit cards — West Bengal, Odisha, Bihar and other state schemes

careers.example_from_field: Dr. Urbasi Sinha of the Raman Research Institute (RRI), Bengaluru, leads India's foremost experimental quantum information science group. She has demonstrated quantum key distribution and entanglement experiments that have received international recognition and contributed to India's National Quantum Mission roadmap.

careers.title: Space Tech Engineer
careers.description: A Space Tech Engineer designs, builds, tests, and operates satellites, launch vehicles, payloads, and ground-station systems. India's space sector was opened to private players through the Indian Space Policy 2023 and the establishment of IN-SPACe, creating a booming ecosystem of startups such as Skyroot Aerospace, Agnikul Cosmos, Pixxel, and Bellatrix Aerospace. Indian students can now pursue careers at ISRO, DRDO, and private new-space companies, and are eligible for international roles at ESA, SpaceX (via US visa routes), and NASA-funded research programmes.
personality_traits: You are fascinated by space exploration and satellite technology
You are highly analytical and detail-oriented about engineering tolerances
You enjoy working in interdisciplinary teams across mechanical, electronics, and software domains
You are patient with long development cycles and rigorous testing protocols
You are motivated by national and global impact
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCM (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics); high scores in Physics and Mathematics are strongly preferred.
entrance_exams: JEE Advanced (for IIT Aerospace Engineering / Engineering Physics)
ISRO Centralised Recruitment Board (ICRB) – Scientist/Engineer SC exam
DRDO Entry Test (CEPTAM / SET) for technical posts
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCM → B.Tech/B.E. in Aerospace Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Electronics, or Avionics (4 years) → Specialise via M.Tech in Space Engineering, Satellite Technology, or Propulsion (2 years, optional but advantageous) → Apply to ISRO Scientist/Engineer SC or join private new-space companies post graduation. Gain hands-on experience through ISRO Student Projects (YUVIKA programme), IIST research internships, or building CubeSats at the student level. IIT alumni regularly join new-space startups and global space agencies.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 50,000 – 2,50,000 per year (government IITs/NITs/IIST); INR 1,50,000 – 5,00,000 per year (private colleges); IIST (Indian Institute of Space Science and Technology) is fully funded by ISRO/Govt. for meritorious students.
careers.career_growth_path: Trainee Engineer → Scientist/Engineer-SC → Scientist/Engineer-SD → Scientist/Engineer-SE → Group Director → Programme Director → Distinguished Scientist / Mission Director

work_environment.places_of_work: ISRO centres (VSSC, URSC, SAC, NRSC, LPSC), DRDO labs, private new-space startups (Skyroot, Agnikul, Pixxel), defence PSUs (HAL, BEL), and academic research centres.
work_environment.work_environment: Highly mission-driven, team-based environment. Launch periods involve high-pressure, round-the-clock work. Day-to-day involves design simulations, testing, and documentation. ISRO culture is collaborative and strongly government-oriented; startups offer faster-paced, equity-driven environments.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: IN-SPACe policy enables Indian entrepreneurs to start satellite manufacturing, launch services, or space data analytics companies.

scholarships: IIST Scholarship – ISRO funds qualified students at IIST with full tuition and stipend
ISRO YUVIKA (Young Scientist Programme) – for Class 9 students to engage with space science
DST-INSPIRE Fellowship for PhD in Physical Sciences
National Scholarship Portal: www.scholarships.gov.in (Central Govt & UGC/AICTE)
Kishore Vaigyanik Protsahan Yojana (KVPY) / SN Bose Scholars Programme
loans: SBI Scholar Loan Scheme (for IITs and IIST admissions)
Education loans from nationalised banks at priority rates

Skyroot has since raised over $68 million and is developing orbital launch vehicles.

careers.title: Health Informatics Specialist
careers.description: A Health Informatics Specialist bridges the gap between healthcare and information technology by designing, implementing, and managing electronic health record (EHR) systems, health data analytics platforms, and digital health infrastructure. In India, the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) — which aims to create a Universal Health ID for every citizen — has created a massive demand for professionals who understand both clinical workflows and IT systems. Indian students can also access international roles at WHO, global hospital chains, and health-tech companies like Practo, MediBuddy, and PharmEasy.
personality_traits: You are interested in both healthcare and technology
You enjoy organising and interpreting large datasets
You care about improving patient outcomes through better data systems
You are detail-oriented and systematic in documenting processes
You are a strong communicator who can translate clinical needs into technical requirements
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream (PCB or PCM preferred); Commerce with mathematics is also acceptable for informatics-focused pathways.
entrance_exams: NEET-UG (for MBBS/BDS/BAMS — clinical route before informatics specialisation)
GATE – Computer Science / Biotechnology / Biomedical Engineering (for M.Tech)
University-specific entrance exams for M.Sc. Health Informatics / M.Tech Biomedical Informatics
CAT / MAT (for MBA in Healthcare Management)
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCB or PCM → Pursue B.Sc. in Health Informatics, Hospital Management, Computer Science, or MBBS/BDS (4-5.5 years) → Specialise with M.Sc./M.Tech in Health Informatics, Biomedical Informatics, or Digital Health (2 years) OR MBA in Hospital and Healthcare Management → Obtain certifications: CPHIMS (Certified Professional in Health Informatics and Information Management), HL7 FHIR Certification, AWS Healthcare Competency → Apply to ABDM implementation partners, hospital groups (Apollo, Fortis, AIIMS), or health-tech startups.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 40,000 – 2,00,000 per year (government institutions); INR 1,50,000 – 5,00,000 per year (private); CPHIMS certification: INR 25,000 – 40,000
careers.career_growth_path: Health IT Analyst → Health Informatics Specialist → Senior Health Informatics Consultant → Health Informatics Manager → Chief Health Information Officer (CHIO) / Chief Digital Health Officer

work_environment.places_of_work: Hospitals (AIIMS, Apollo, Fortis, Medanta), health-tech startups (Practo, MediBuddy, PharmEasy), government health bodies (NHA — National Health Authority), insurance companies, WHO, and IT service firms (TCS Health, HCL Healthcare).
work_environment.work_environment: Office-based with regular coordination with clinical and IT teams. May visit hospital wards to understand workflows. Hybrid working increasingly common. Government roles offer stability; startup roles offer faster growth and equity.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can build a health-tech startup focused on EHR, telemedicine interoperability, or ABDM-compliant solutions.

Dr. Ambedkar Post-Matric Scholarship (Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment)
State health sector scholarships (varies by state — Maharashtra, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu offer specific healthcare scholarships)

His work in integrating health informatics into national health policy has directly created thousands of Health Informatics specialist roles across India.

careers.title: Supply Chain Analyst
careers.description: A Supply Chain Analyst uses data analysis, modelling, and optimisation tools to improve the flow of goods, materials, and information from supplier to customer. In India, with e-commerce platforms (Flipkart, Amazon, Meesho) and the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan investing billions in logistics infrastructure, supply chain analytics is one of the fastest-growing professional domains. The role requires proficiency in tools like SAP SCM, Oracle SCM, Python/R for analytics, and Power BI or Tableau for visualisation. Indian students are well-positioned for both domestic roles (Delhivery, BlueDart, Mahindra Logistics) and global MNC positions.
personality_traits: You enjoy analysing data to find efficiency improvements
You are highly organised and enjoy planning complex sequences
You are good at spotting bottlenecks and optimisation opportunities
You are comfortable working with both numbers and cross-functional teams
You are adaptable — supply chains involve constant change
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Commerce with Mathematics or Science stream with PCM; Mathematics is essential.
entrance_exams: CAT / XAT / GMAT (for MBA in Operations Management / Supply Chain)
NMAT / SNAP / MAT (for MBA at private B-schools)
GATE – Industrial Engineering / Mechanical Engineering (for M.Tech Operations)
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with Mathematics → B.Tech in Industrial Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or B.Com/BBA (3-4 years) → MBA in Operations/Supply Chain Management or M.Tech Industrial Engineering (2 years) → Obtain certifications: APICS CSCP (Certified Supply Chain Professional), APICS CPIM, or Google Supply Chain Analytics Certificate → Join logistics companies, FMCG firms, e-commerce companies, or consulting firms as an analyst.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 60,000 – 3,00,000 per year (government B-schools/NITs); INR 5,00,000 – 25,00,000 for top MBA programmes (IIMs, XLRI); APICS CSCP certification: INR 30,000 – 55,000
careers.career_growth_path: Supply Chain Analyst → Senior Analyst → Supply Chain Manager → Supply Chain Director → VP of Operations / Chief Supply Chain Officer (CSCO)

work_environment.places_of_work: FMCG companies (HUL, ITC, P&G India), e-commerce (Amazon, Flipkart), logistics (Delhivery, BlueDart, Mahindra Logistics), manufacturing (Tata, Maruti), consulting (McKinsey, Deloitte, Accenture Supply Chain practice).
work_environment.work_environment: Primarily office-based with occasional visits to warehouses or supplier sites. Collaborative environment with cross-functional exposure to procurement, warehouse, and finance teams. Fast-paced in e-commerce; more structured in manufacturing.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a supply chain consulting firm or a logistics technology startup (e.g., route optimisation, warehouse automation).

scholarships: IIM scholarships and fee waivers based on merit and financial need
APICS Foundation Scholarship (for APICS certifications — open to Indian applicants)
Aditya Birla Group Scholarship (for IIM and other premier institution students)
loans: SBI Scholar Loan for IIMs and premier B-schools

HDFC Credila, Avanse, InCred for MBA programmes
NBFCs and fintech lenders for certification courses

careers.title: EdTech Product Designer
careers.description: An EdTech Product Designer creates engaging, research-backed digital learning experiences for students and professionals. They combine principles of instructional design, user experience (UX), learning science, and visual design to build courses, apps, simulations, and interactive modules. India's EdTech sector — home to BYJU'S, Unacademy, Vedantu, Toppr, upGrad, and hundreds of funded startups — is one of the world's largest and demands designers who understand both pedagogy and product development. Indian students can also access remote roles at global EdTech firms like Coursera, Khan Academy, and Duolingo.
personality_traits: You are passionate about making learning engaging and accessible
You are creative with a strong visual sense and attention to UX details
You enjoy researching how people learn and retain information
You are comfortable working at the intersection of education, design, and technology
You are collaborative and empathetic toward diverse learner needs
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream (Arts, Commerce, or Science); a background in education or design is helpful but not mandatory.
entrance_exams: NATA / JEE Paper 2 (for B.Arch / B.Des at NITs and architecture colleges)
NID Entrance Exam (for National Institute of Design, Ahmedabad)
CEED (Common Entrance Examination for Design — for M.Des at IITs and IISc)
UCEED (for B.Des at IIT Bombay, IIT Delhi, IIT Guwahati)
University-specific entrance tests for BEd, MEd, or instructional design programmes
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 in any stream → Pursue B.Des in Interaction Design / Communication Design, or B.Tech in CSE with a UX minor, or BA/B.Sc. in Education (3-4 years) → Specialise with M.Des in Interaction Design, PG Diploma in Instructional Design, or M.Ed (2 years) → Build a portfolio of learning product designs → Apply to EdTech companies as a UX Designer, Instructional Designer, or Learning Experience Designer → Transition to EdTech Product Designer with 2-3 years of experience.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 50,000 – 2,50,000 per year (government NID, IIT design schools); INR 1,50,000 – 6,00,000 per year (private design colleges); online instructional design certifications: INR 15,000 – 50,000
careers.career_growth_path: Instructional Designer → UX Designer (EdTech) → EdTech Product Designer → Senior Product Designer → Lead Designer → Head of Product Design / VP of Product (Learning)

work_environment.places_of_work: EdTech companies (BYJU'S, Unacademy, Vedantu, upGrad, Eruditus, Skill-Lync), school/university digital learning teams, corporate L&D departments, NGOs (Pratham, Teach for India), and remote roles at international EdTech firms.
work_environment.work_environment: Collaborative, creative environment using tools like Figma, Adobe XD, Articulate Storyline, and Camtasia. Sprint-based agile product teams. Largely office or hybrid; strong remote culture at many EdTech firms.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can launch an independent instructional design studio, create and sell courses on Udemy/Teachable, or build a niche EdTech startup.

AICTE Pragati Scholarship for Girl Students (for design and engineering)

careers.title: Computational Linguist
careers.description: A Computational Linguist develops algorithms and models to enable computers to understand, process, and generate human language. They work at the intersection of linguistics, computer science, and artificial intelligence — building tools like language models, speech recognition systems, machine translation engines, and chatbots. India, with 22 scheduled languages and hundreds of dialects, presents a unique and pressing need for Indic NLP (Natural Language Processing) research. Companies like Google India (Indic language support), Microsoft India, Sarvam AI, AI4Bharat, and hundreds of startups are actively hiring computational linguists to make AI work in Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Bengali, and other Indian languages.
personality_traits: You are fascinated by how language works and how meaning is created
You enjoy both theoretical linguistics and applied software development
You are analytical and comfortable with statistical modelling
You enjoy research and can communicate complex linguistic findings clearly
You care about language inclusivity and making technology accessible in regional languages
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Science with PCM is advantageous for the computational side, but Arts/Humanities students with strong aptitude for coding can equally enter via linguistics pathways.
entrance_exams: JEE Main / JEE Advanced (for B.Tech CSE — later specialising in NLP/AI)
CUET UG / University entrance exams (for BA Linguistics at central universities)
GATE – Computer Science (for M.Tech AI/NLP at IITs and NITs)
TOEFL/GRE (for international MS in Computational Linguistics — US/Europe)
University-specific entrance tests for M.A./M.Sc. Linguistics
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 → Pursue BA in Linguistics or B.Tech in CSE (3-4 years) → Specialise with M.A. in Computational Linguistics or M.Tech/M.Sc. in Artificial Intelligence with NLP focus (2 years) → Build skills in Python (NLTK, spaCy, Hugging Face Transformers), and contribute to open-source Indic NLP projects (AI4Bharat, IndicNLP) → Apply at tech companies, research labs, or pursue PhD for academic career. IIT Madras, IIT Bombay, and JNU are leading centres for computational linguistics research in India.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 20,000 – 1,50,000 per year (government universities — BA/MA Linguistics); INR 50,000 – 2,50,000 per year (IIT M.Tech programmes); Hugging Face and fast.ai NLP courses: free online
careers.career_growth_path: NLP Research Intern → NLP Engineer → Senior NLP Engineer → Computational Linguist / Research Scientist → Lead NLP Scientist → Principal Scientist / Head of Language AI Research

work_environment.places_of_work: Tech companies (Google India, Microsoft India, Amazon Alexa India, Meta AI India), NLP startups (Sarvam AI, Slang Labs, Vernacular.ai), academic research labs (IIT Madras AI4Bharat, C-DAC), and global remote roles.
work_environment.work_environment: Research-oriented and collaborative. Mix of independent deep-work (model training, analysis) and team collaboration (product integration, annotation). Largely office or remote-friendly.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can build a language technology startup focused on Indic language AI, speech-to-text, or machine translation for regional markets.

scholarships: National Scholarship Portal: www.scholarships.gov.in (UGC & Central Govt schemes)
UGC-NET JRF (Junior Research Fellowship) for Linguistics PhD students
DST-INSPIRE Fellowship for PhD in Computational Sciences
Google PhD Fellowship Programme (open to Indian researchers in AI/ML/NLP)
TCS Research Scholarship for AI/NLP research at IITs
loans: SBI Scholar Loan for IIT admissions

IIT Bombay – CSE / Language Technology Group

EFLU (English and Foreign Languages University), Hyderabad
C-DAC (Centre for Development of Advanced Computing) — Technology Development for Indian Languages (TDIL)

Amity University – MA Linguistics / NLP courses
Shiv Nadar University – CSE with AI/NLP electives
distance_learning: Coursera: NLP Specialisation by deeplearning.ai (available to Indian students)
AI4Bharat open-source project (practical Indic NLP — accessible to all)

careers.example_from_field: Prof. Mitesh Khapra, Associate Professor at IIT Madras and co-founder of AI4Bharat, leads the development of IndicBERT and IndicGLUE — foundational language models for 11 Indian languages. His work has been adopted by technology companies across India and has positioned IIT Madras as a global leader in Indic NLP research.
careers.source: AI4Bharat (IIT Madras, 2024); Glassdoor India NLP Engineer salaries (2025); C-DAC TDIL programme reports; India Skills Report 2025; ACL Anthology (2024 papers citing Indian institutions)

careers.title: Mental Health Counsellor
careers.description: A Mental Health Counsellor provides professional counselling, psychotherapy, and psychological support to individuals experiencing mental health challenges including anxiety, depression, relationship issues, trauma, grief, and addiction. In India, the Mental Healthcare Act 2017 and the growing awareness post-COVID-19 have significantly expanded the demand for qualified counsellors. Platforms like iCall, YourDOST, Practo, and Vandrevala Foundation are creating digital-first roles. Indian students with a recognised qualification can also work internationally — in the UK (with BACP accreditation), UAE, Singapore, and Canada.
personality_traits: You are empathetic and genuinely interested in people's emotional wellbeing
You are a patient and non-judgmental listener
You can maintain professional boundaries while building trust
You are emotionally resilient and have good self-care practices
You are committed to continuous learning in psychology and counselling methods
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Science with Biology is preferred for the clinical psychology pathway. Arts with Psychology is equally valid.
entrance_exams: CUET UG (for BA Psychology at central universities)
University-specific entrance exams for B.Sc. / BA Psychology
CUET PG / university entrance exams for M.Sc./MA Clinical Psychology
RCI (Rehabilitation Council of India) Examination for Clinical Psychologist registration
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 → Pursue BA/B.Sc. in Psychology (3 years) → Complete M.Sc./MA in Clinical Psychology or Counselling Psychology (2 years) → Obtain M.Phil in Clinical Psychology (2 years) — this is currently mandated by the Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI) for full clinical registration → Register with RCI as a Rehabilitation Psychologist / Clinical Psychologist → Gain supervised clinical hours (minimum 500 hours) → Apply at hospitals, NGOs, schools, corporates, or private practice. Note: The National Commission for Allied and Healthcare Professions Act 2021 is creating a new regulatory framework that may update licensure requirements.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 20,000 – 1,00,000 per year (government universities — BA/MA/M.Phil); INR 80,000 – 3,00,000 per year (private institutions); RCI registration fee: approximately INR 2,000–5,000
careers.career_growth_path: Intern Counsellor (supervised) → Counsellor → Senior Counsellor → Lead Therapist / Clinical Supervisor → Head of Counselling Services / Chief Mental Health Officer

work_environment.places_of_work: Hospitals and psychiatric centres (NIMHANS, AIIMS psychiatry, Apollo), schools and colleges (student counselling cells), corporates (Employee Assistance Programmes), NGOs (iCall, Vandrevala Foundation), online therapy platforms (YourDOST, Wysa), and private practice.
work_environment.work_environment: Client-facing, confidential, and emotionally demanding. Sessions are typically 45-60 minutes. Requires strict ethical standards. Private practice offers flexibility; institutional roles offer stability. Hybrid and telehealth counselling has grown significantly post-COVID.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can establish a private practice clinic, a group therapy centre, or an online mental health startup.

scholarships: National Scholarship Portal: www.scholarships.gov.in (UGC & Central Govt schemes)
NIMHANS Fellowships for M.Phil Clinical Psychology students
UGC-NET JRF for Psychology PhD research
State mental health mission scholarships (varies by state)
Vandrevala Foundation scholarship for psychology students (Maharashtra)
loans: HDFC Credila for postgraduate psychology programmes

AIIMS New Delhi – Department of Psychiatry (M.Phil Clinical Psychology)
