She has been featured in The Hindu, NDTV, and speaks nationally on breaking the stigma around seeking counselling.
careers.source: Mental Healthcare Act 2017 (MoHFW); Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI) registration guidelines; NIMHANS official website; Glassdoor India Clinical Psychologist salaries (2025); National Mental Health Survey of India 2015-16 (NIMHANS/MoHFW)

careers.title: Geospatial Analyst
careers.description: A Geospatial Analyst collects, processes, and interprets spatial data from satellites, drones, GPS systems, and remote sensors to create maps, models, and location intelligence for applications in urban planning, disaster management, agriculture, defence, and environmental monitoring. India's National Remote Sensing Centre (NRSC/ISRO), Survey of India, and the proliferating GIS industry — valued at over $2 billion in India — create strong domestic demand. Companies like Esri India, MapmyIndia, Trimble, and urban analytics startups all hire geospatial analysts. Global roles at WHO, World Bank, UNDP, and climate organisations are also accessible to Indian graduates.
personality_traits: You are naturally drawn to maps, geography, and spatial thinking
You are analytical and enjoy working with satellite and sensor data
You are curious about how location influences everything from disease spread to crop yields
You have an eye for visual data representation and cartographic storytelling
You are comfortable working with both field data collection and software analysis
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCM or Geography as a subject (in Arts stream) is helpful; Mathematics is essential.
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech in Geomatics / Civil Engineering / Geo-informatics at NITs)
GATE – Geology and Geophysics / Civil Engineering (for M.Tech Geomatics/GIS)
CUET PG / University entrance exams for M.Sc. Remote Sensing and GIS
IIRS (Indian Institute of Remote Sensing, Dehradun) — ISRO's specialised PG Diploma entrance
Civil Services Examination (Geography optional — urban planning roles)
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCM or Arts/Geography → B.Tech in Geomatics Engineering, Civil Engineering, or B.Sc. in Geography / Environmental Science (3-4 years) → Specialise with M.Sc. in Remote Sensing and GIS or M.Tech in Geomatics Engineering (2 years) → Gain proficiency in ArcGIS, QGIS, Google Earth Engine, Python (geopandas, rasterio), and drone photogrammetry → Apply at ISRO/NRSC, Survey of India, MapmyIndia, Esri India, or urban planning agencies. IIRS Dehradun (ISRO's training institute) offers highly regarded PG Diploma programmes.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 30,000 – 1,50,000 per year (government institutions); INR 80,000 – 3,00,000 per year (private colleges); IIRS ISRO PG Diploma: approximately INR 30,000–80,000
careers.career_growth_path: GIS Technician → GIS Analyst / Geospatial Analyst → Senior Geospatial Analyst → GIS Manager → Director of Geospatial Intelligence / Chief GIS Officer

work_environment.places_of_work: ISRO/NRSC (Hyderabad), Survey of India, Smart Cities Mission agencies, MapmyIndia, Esri India, defence/intelligence agencies, UN agencies (UNDP, FAO, WHO), NGOs (WRI India), agricultural analytics startups, and climate research organisations.
work_environment.work_environment: Office and lab-based for software analysis; occasional field missions for data collection. Collaborative environment involving planners, engineers, and policymakers. Government roles offer stability; private sector offers higher pay and faster growth.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a geospatial data company, a drone mapping service, or a precision agriculture analytics platform.

scholarships: DST-INSPIRE Fellowship (for PhD in Earth Sciences / Remote Sensing)
IIRS-ISRO PG Training Scholarship (sponsored seats for meritorious students)
CSIR-UGC JRF (Earth, Atmospheric, Ocean and Planetary Sciences)
Esri Education Programme grants (GIS software and learning access for students)
loans: HDFC Credila and Avanse for postgraduate studies

government: Indian Institute of Remote Sensing (IIRS), Dehradun, Uttarakhand (ISRO) – PG Diploma / M.Tech Remote Sensing and GIS
Andhra University, Visakhapatnam – M.Sc. Applied Remote Sensing and GIS
Banaras Hindu University (BHU) – M.Sc. Geography and Remote Sensing
private: Birla Institute of Technology (BIT Mesra), Ranchi – M.Tech Remote Sensing
Amity University – M.Sc. Remote Sensing and GIS
distance_learning: Coursera: GIS, Mapping, and Spatial Analysis Specialisation (University of California Davis)
Esri MOOC Programme (ArcGIS training — free for students)

careers.example_from_field: Dr. V.K. Dadhwal, former Director of the National Remote Sensing Centre (NRSC) Hyderabad, pioneered the use of satellite-based crop yield estimation and urban heat island mapping across India. His geospatial models directly contributed to India's food security analytics and climate vulnerability assessments used by the Ministry of Agriculture.
careers.source: NRSC-ISRO official website; IIRS Dehradun programme catalogue; Glassdoor India GIS Analyst salaries (2025); India GIS Market Report (Mordor Intelligence 2024); Survey of India recruitment notifications

careers.title: Legal Tech Consultant
careers.description: A Legal Tech Consultant advises law firms, corporate legal departments, and legal tech startups on the adoption, implementation, and optimisation of technology solutions including contract management software, AI-powered legal research tools (like LegalEdge, SCC Online AI), document automation, e-discovery platforms, and case management systems. India's ₹1.4 trillion legal services market is undergoing digital transformation, with startups like Legistify, Leegality (e-signing), and ContractPodAi hiring professionals who combine legal knowledge with tech expertise. Indian legal tech consultants can also work with global law firms in the UK, US, and Singapore.
personality_traits: You are analytically strong with an interest in both law and technology
You enjoy problem-solving at the intersection of process, compliance, and software
You are detail-oriented and can translate complex legal requirements into system specifications
You are a strong communicator who can work with both lawyers and software developers
You are entrepreneurial and excited about modernising a traditionally conservative profession
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Arts/Commerce/Science are all valid entry points. Humanities or Science backgrounds are equally suitable.
entrance_exams: CLAT (Common Law Admission Test) – for National Law Universities (NLUs) — 5-year BA LLB
AILET (All India Law Entrance Test) – for NLU Delhi
State-level law entrance exams (MH CET Law, AP LAWCET, TS LAWCET)
CAT / GMAT (for MBA in Legal Management or Business — combined with law background)
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 → Pursue 5-year integrated BA LLB or BBA LLB at a National Law University or recognised law school → Specialise in Corporate Law, IP Law, or Technology Law → Pursue LLM in Technology Law / Cyber Law (1 year) OR take additional courses in data analytics, project management, or enterprise software → Obtain relevant certifications (Contract Lifecycle Management – CLM, IAPP CIPM for privacy law tech) → Join a legal tech startup, law firm's technology group, or a Big 4 (Deloitte Legal, KPMG Legal Tech, EY Law) as a legal tech analyst/consultant.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 1,00,000 – 2,50,000 per year (National Law Universities); INR 2,00,000 – 6,00,000 per year (private law schools); LLM: INR 80,000 – 2,50,000; CLM/IAPP certifications: INR 20,000 – 60,000
careers.career_growth_path: Legal Tech Analyst → Legal Tech Consultant → Senior Legal Tech Consultant → Legal Technology Manager → Head of Legal Operations / Chief Legal Technology Officer (CLTO)

work_environment.places_of_work: Legal tech startups (Legistify, Leegality, ContractPodAi India), law firms' technology and innovation groups, Big 4 legal consulting arms (Deloitte Legal, KPMG Legal Tech), corporate legal departments (Tata, Reliance, Infosys), and Bar Council-approved legal innovation centres.
work_environment.work_environment: Office-based with project-based client engagements. Mix of strategy work, software demonstrations, workflow design, and training. Dynamic and growing environment. Hybrid work common at startups; structured at Big 4 and law firms.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: High — can found a legal tech startup focused on contract automation, AI legal research, or compliance management.

scholarships: National Scholarship Portal: www.scholarships.gov.in (Central Govt & UGC schemes for law)
NLU-specific merit scholarships and financial aid programmes
Bar Council of India Trust Scholarship
CLAT Consortium NLU Fee Waiver Schemes (for economically weaker students)
Rhodes Scholarship / Chevening (for LLM abroad — open to Indian lawyers with strong profile)
loans: Education loans from nationalised banks (law schools eligible)
HDFC Credila for NLU and LLM programmes
Avanse and InCred for law school financing

government: National Law School of India University (NLSIU), Bengaluru – BA LLB / LLM
private: Symbiosis Law School, Pune – BA LLB / LLM Technology Law
UPES School of Law, Dehradun – BBA LLB Energy and Technology Law
Bennett University, Greater Noida – School of Law
distance_learning: IGNOU – BA LLB / LLM (Law — distance mode)
Coursera: Legal Tech and the Future of Law (Duke University — available to Indian students)
Stanford CodeX FutureLaw conference (annual online access for global legal tech professionals)

careers.example_from_field: Akshay Verma, co-founder of Leegality (India's first digital signature and e-stamping platform), an NLU Jodhpur alumnus, has automated more than 10 million agreements for companies including PhonePe, CreditMantri, and Tata Capital. Leegality is now used by over 700 enterprises and is a leading example of a homegrown Indian legal tech success story.
careers.source: Bar Council of India legal tech policy statements; Leegality company website; Glassdoor India Legal Consultant salaries (2025); India Legal Services Market Report (Nasscom 2024); CLAT Consortium NLU fee structure 2024-25

careers.title: Regulatory Affairs Specialist
careers.description: A Regulatory Affairs Specialist ensures that pharmaceutical drugs, medical devices, biotechnology products, and food items comply with the regulations of national bodies (CDSCO — Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation, FSSAI, BIS) and international bodies (US FDA, EMA, ICH). India is the world's third-largest pharmaceutical producer by volume and the largest supplier of generic medicines globally, creating enormous demand for regulatory professionals at companies like Sun Pharma, Cipla, Dr. Reddy's, Lupin, and Biocon. Indian specialists with knowledge of US FDA and EU EMA regulations are particularly sought after for global drug submissions.
personality_traits: You are highly detail-oriented and systematic in documentation
You enjoy interpreting complex regulatory guidelines and applying them to products
You are a strong communicator who can liaise with both scientific teams and government agencies
You are persistent and can navigate bureaucratic processes patiently
You are updated on evolving regulations and compliance requirements
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCB (Physics, Chemistry, Biology) is strongly preferred; PCM is also acceptable for device-focused roles.
entrance_exams: NEET-UG (for B.Pharm/M.Pharm via some pharmacy colleges)
GPAT (Graduate Pharmacy Aptitude Test) – for M.Pharm admission at government colleges
GATE – Biotechnology / Life Sciences (for M.Tech roles in biotech regulatory affairs)
University-specific entrance exams for M.Sc. Regulatory Affairs / Clinical Research
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCB/PCM → B.Pharm (4 years) or B.Sc. in Life Sciences / Biochemistry / Biotechnology (3 years) → Pursue M.Pharm in Regulatory Affairs / Pharmacology, or M.Sc. in Regulatory Affairs and Clinical Research (2 years) → Obtain certifications: RAC (Regulatory Affairs Certification by RAPS), CCRP (Certified Clinical Research Professional), or Indian RA certification through IRAP → Apply to pharmaceutical or medical device companies' regulatory affairs departments.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 30,000 – 1,00,000 per year (government pharmacy colleges); INR 80,000 – 3,00,000 per year (private colleges); RAC certification by RAPS: INR 40,000 – 70,000
careers.career_growth_path: Regulatory Affairs Executive → Regulatory Affairs Officer → Senior Regulatory Affairs Specialist → Regulatory Affairs Manager → Head of Regulatory Affairs → VP Regulatory and Medical Affairs

work_environment.places_of_work: Pharmaceutical companies (Sun Pharma, Cipla, Dr. Reddy's, Lupin, Biocon, Zydus), medical device companies (Medtronic India, Abbott India), CDSCO (Central Government), FSSAI, CROs (clinical research organisations like Quintiles, Parexel India), and international regulatory consulting firms.
work_environment.work_environment: Office-based with documentation-intensive work. Requires close coordination with R&D, quality assurance, and manufacturing teams. Occasional interaction with regulators. Deadline-driven during product submissions.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a regulatory affairs consulting firm offering submission services to smaller pharma and biotech startups.

scholarships: National Scholarship Portal: www.scholarships.gov.in (AICTE/UGC Pharmacy scholarships)
RAPS Foundation Scholarship (for RAPS members pursuing RAC — open to Indian applicants)
Department of Pharmaceuticals (DoP) scholarships for pharma students
loans: Education loans from nationalised banks (B.Pharm / M.Pharm eligible)
HDFC Credila and Avanse for pharmacy postgraduate programmes

Jamia Hamdard University, New Delhi – M.Pharm Regulatory Affairs
Manipal College of Pharmaceutical Sciences, Manipal
JSS College of Pharmacy, Mysuru (Government Aided)

MIT School of Bioengineering Sciences and Research, Pune
NIMS University, Jaipur – M.Pharm Regulatory Affairs

RAPS (Regulatory Affairs Professionals Society) online RAC prep courses
Coursera: Drug Development Product Management Specialisation (UC San Diego — available to Indian students)

careers.example_from_field: Kiran Mazumdar Shaw, Chairperson of Biocon (Bengaluru), built India's largest biopharmaceutical company by navigating complex US FDA and EMA regulatory processes to get biosimilars approved internationally. Her regulatory strategy team's work in achieving trastuzumab (Herceptin biosimilar) FDA approval in 2019 created a new global benchmark for Indian pharma regulatory excellence.
careers.source: CDSCO official regulatory guidelines; RAPS RAC certification body; Glassdoor India Regulatory Affairs salaries (2025); Indian Pharmaceutical Alliance (IPA) Industry Report 2024; Pharmexcil India export statistics 2023-24

careers.title: User Researcher
careers.description: A User Researcher systematically studies how people interact with products, services, and digital interfaces to generate insights that drive product decisions. Using methods like interviews, usability testing, surveys, A/B testing, eye-tracking, and ethnographic research, user researchers help companies understand user needs, pain points, and mental models. In India, major tech companies (Flipkart, Swiggy, Zomato, Paytm, Google India, Microsoft India) and UX-mature startups have built dedicated user research functions. Indian researchers with knowledge of India's diverse urban-rural digital divide are particularly valued.
personality_traits: You are deeply curious about why people behave the way they do
You are an empathetic listener who can put users at ease during interviews
You are rigorous and systematic in your research methodology
You are a storyteller who can translate raw observations into compelling insights
You are collaborative and influence product decisions through evidence, not hierarchy
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Psychology or Social Sciences background is particularly useful. Science (PCM) students who develop human behaviour interests also make excellent user researchers.
entrance_exams: UCEED (for B.Des at IIT Bombay/Delhi/Guwahati — includes interaction design)
CEED (for M.Des at IITs and IISc)
NID Entrance Exam (for NID Design programmes)
CUET UG / University entrance for BA/B.Sc. Psychology
CAT / Management entrance exams for MBA product management pathways
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 → Pursue B.Sc. in Psychology, B.Des in Interaction Design, B.Tech in CSE, or BA in Sociology/Anthropology (3-4 years) → Specialise via M.Des in Interaction Design, M.Sc. in HCI (Human-Computer Interaction), or online bootcamps in UX Research (Nielsen Norman Group, Google UX Certificate) → Build a portfolio of 3-5 research case studies → Apply to tech companies as a UX Research Intern → transition to User Researcher role. Nielsen Norman Group's UX Certification is globally respected and accessible online.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 50,000 – 2,50,000 per year (government design schools/IITs); INR 1,50,000 – 6,00,000 per year (private); Google UX Research Professional Certificate (Coursera): INR 3,000–5,000/month
careers.career_growth_path: UX Research Intern → Junior User Researcher → User Researcher → Senior User Researcher → Principal Researcher → Director of User Research / Head of Design Research

work_environment.places_of_work: Tech companies (Google India, Microsoft India, Swiggy, Flipkart, Paytm, PhonePe), design agencies, FMCG research teams, UX consultancies, NGOs conducting human-centred design projects.
work_environment.work_environment: A mix of field visits (user interviews at homes or workplaces), lab-based usability testing, and data analysis at desk. Collaborative with product managers and designers. Largely office or hybrid with occasional field research travel.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a UX research agency or a human-centred design consultancy serving startups and corporates.

scholarships: National Scholarship Portal: www.scholarships.gov.in (Design / Arts scholarships)
National Design Scholarship (NID / Ministry of Commerce and Industry)
AICTE Pragati Scholarship for Girl Students
Google Career Certificates Financial Aid (for UX Certificate on Coursera — covers full cost for eligible students)
loans: HDFC Credila for design school tuition
GrayQuest and Propelld for bootcamp/short-course financing

government: National Institute of Design (NID), Ahmedabad – Interaction Design / M.Des
IIT Bombay – IDC School of Design (B.Des / M.Des Interaction Design)
IIT Delhi – Design Department (M.Des Human Factors)
IISc Bengaluru – M.Des (2-year programme with HCI focus)

careers.example_from_field: Rajashree Shreenivasa, Principal UX Researcher at Flipkart (Bengaluru), led foundational research on how first-time internet users in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities navigate e-commerce on low-cost Android phones. Her ethnographic research directly shaped Flipkart's vernacular language interface and voice search features, expanding the platform's reach to over 50 million non-English speaking users.
careers.source: Nielsen Norman Group UX Salary Survey 2024; Glassdoor India UX Researcher salaries (2025); NID Ahmedabad M.Des programme brochure; India Product Market Report (iSPIRT 2024)

careers.title: Carbon Credit Analyst
careers.description: A Carbon Credit Analyst evaluates, quantifies, verifies, and trades carbon credits — financial instruments representing the reduction or removal of one tonne of CO2 equivalent. India's Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS), notified under the Energy Conservation (Amendment) Act 2022, and SEBI's framework for ESG-linked instruments are rapidly creating a new professional category. Globally, the voluntary carbon market is projected to grow to $50 billion by 2030. Indian students with knowledge of carbon accounting, GHG Protocol, and SEBI/BEE compliance can work in India's National Carbon Market or in international voluntary carbon markets (Verra, Gold Standard).
personality_traits: You are passionate about climate action and environmental sustainability
You are analytically strong and comfortable with quantitative data and financial modelling
You enjoy working in a fast-evolving policy-driven market
You are detail-oriented in documentation and verification tasks
You are comfortable liaising between project developers, auditors, and financial markets
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream (PCB, PCM, or Environmental Science) or Commerce with Mathematics; both pathways lead to the role through different specialisations.
entrance_exams: CUET PG / University entrance exams for M.Sc. Environmental Science / Climate Studies
CAT / MAT (for MBA in Sustainability / Environmental Management)
GATE – Environmental Science and Engineering (for M.Tech)
University-specific tests for M.Sc. Environmental Economics
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with Science or Commerce → B.Sc. in Environmental Science, Chemistry, or B.Tech in Environmental Engineering, OR B.Com/BBA (3-4 years) → Specialise with M.Sc. in Environmental Science, Climate Policy, or MBA in Sustainability (2 years) → Obtain GHG Protocol certification, Verra VCS Project Developer Training, or GoldStandard Registry certification → Build experience through carbon project internships at project developers, environmental consulting firms, or SEBI-registered ESG agencies → Apply to carbon credit firms, energy companies, or sustainability consultancies.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 30,000 – 1,50,000 per year (government colleges); INR 80,000 – 4,00,000 per year (private); GHG Protocol online course: free; Verra VCS training: approximately INR 15,000–30,000
careers.career_growth_path: Carbon Analyst Intern → Carbon Credit Analyst → Senior Carbon Analyst → Carbon Portfolio Manager → Head of Carbon Markets / Chief Sustainability Officer (CSO)

work_environment.places_of_work: Carbon trading firms, ESG consulting firms (EY Sustainability, Deloitte Sustainability, KPMG Climate), energy companies (NTPC, Greenko, ReNew Power), Indian Carbon Market (BEE/MoPNG), SEBI-registered ESG Rating Agencies, and international carbon credit developers (South Pole, ClimatePartner).
work_environment.work_environment: Office-based research, project analysis, and financial modelling. Occasional field visits to project sites. Collaborative with environmental scientists, auditors, and commodity traders. Fast-evolving regulatory environment requires constant upskilling.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can develop carbon offset projects (reforestation, clean cookstoves, renewable energy) and sell credits on voluntary markets. Can also start a carbon accounting advisory firm.

scholarships: National Scholarship Portal: www.scholarships.gov.in (Environmental Science scholarships)
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) fellowship programmes
DST-INSPIRE Fellowship (for PhD in Earth Sciences / Environmental Science)
TERI (The Energy and Resources Institute) M.Sc. Fellowships
loans: HDFC Credila and Avanse for MBA/M.Sc. programmes

government: TERI School of Advanced Studies (TERI-SAS), New Delhi – M.Sc. Sustainable Development / Climate Policy
IIT Delhi – Centre for Atmospheric Sciences / Environmental Studies
Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU) – School of Environmental Sciences
University of Hyderabad – M.Sc. Environmental Sciences
Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE), Ministry of Power – Carbon Market training programmes

Great Lakes Institute of Management – MBA Sustainability
Amity School of Natural Resources and Sustainable Development
distance_learning: Coursera: Climate Change and Health (Johns Hopkins) / Carbon Markets (Imperial College London)

careers.example_from_field: Sumant Sinha, Chairman and CEO of ReNew Power (New Delhi), is a leading figure in India's clean energy and carbon market landscape. ReNew Power, with over 9 GW of renewable capacity, has generated millions of certified emission reductions (CERs) and is one of India's most active participants in both domestic and international carbon credit markets.
careers.source: Energy Conservation (Amendment) Act 2022 (BEE/MoPNG); SEBI ESG framework circular 2023; Glassdoor India ESG/Sustainability Analyst salaries (2025); Voluntary Carbon Market Integrity Initiative (VCMI) 2024 report; India Carbon Market Development Report (CEEW 2023)

careers.title: Talent Analytics Specialist
careers.description: A Talent Analytics Specialist uses data science, statistical modelling, and HR information systems to help organisations make evidence-based decisions about hiring, workforce planning, employee retention, learning and development, and compensation. Also called a People Analytics professional, this role combines HR domain knowledge with data skills (Python, R, SQL, Tableau, Power BI). India's large IT sector — with companies like TCS, Infosys, Wipro, and Cognizant managing hundreds of thousands of employees — and the startup ecosystem both have growing people analytics teams. Indian specialists are also hired by global HR analytics platforms like Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, and Oracle HCM.
personality_traits: You are analytically strong and enjoy working with large employee data sets
You are curious about human behaviour in organisational contexts
You are a strong communicator who can present data insights to non-technical HR leaders
You balance quantitative rigour with sensitivity to employee privacy and ethics
You enjoy building HR dashboards and predictive attrition or hiring models
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Commerce with Mathematics or Science stream with PCM; strong aptitude for statistics and data analysis is essential.
entrance_exams: CAT / XAT / GMAT (for MBA in HR or Analytics at IIMs/XLRI/TISS)
NMAT / SNAP / MAT (for MBA at private B-schools with analytics specialisation)
GATE – Statistics / Mathematics / Computer Science (for M.Tech / M.Sc. analytics route)
TISS entrance exam (for MA Human Resource Management and Labour Relations at TISS Mumbai)
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with Mathematics → B.Tech in CSE/IT, B.Sc. in Statistics, or BBA/B.Com (3-4 years) → MBA in HR / Analytics at a reputed B-school, OR M.Sc. in Applied Statistics or Data Science (2 years) → Gain proficiency in Excel (Power Query), SQL, Python (pandas), R, Power BI or Tableau, and HR platforms (Workday, SAP SuccessFactors) → Apply as People Analytics Analyst at a tech company or consulting firm → grow into a specialist or manager role within 3-5 years.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 60,000 – 3,00,000 per year (government B-schools/IITs/IIMs); INR 3,00,000 – 20,00,000 for top MBA programmes; online people analytics courses: INR 5,000 – 30,000
careers.career_growth_path: HR Analyst → People Analytics Analyst → Talent Analytics Specialist → Senior People Analytics Manager → Head of People Analytics → Chief People Officer (CPO) or VP Human Resources

work_environment.places_of_work: Large IT companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Cognizant), unicorn startups (Zepto, Meesho, PhonePe), HR consulting firms (Aon Hewitt India, Mercer India, Deloitte Human Capital), HRIS software companies (Darwinbox, Keka HR — Indian HRtech startups), and global MNCs.
work_environment.work_environment: Primarily office or hybrid. Data-intensive desk work combined with presentations to HR leadership. Cross-functional with finance, legal, and business unit leads. Strong culture of confidentiality around employee data.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start an HR analytics consulting firm or build an AI-powered HR platform.

TISS Mumbai Scholarships and Fee Waiver Scheme
IIM scholarships and fee waivers (based on merit and financial need)
Aditya Birla Group Scholarship for MBA students
loans: SBI Scholar Loan for IIMs and top B-schools
HDFC Credila, InCred for MBA financing

careers.title: Robotic Process Automation (RPA) Developer
careers.description: An RPA Developer designs, builds, and deploys software robots (bots) that automate repetitive, rule-based business processes such as data entry, invoice processing, payroll management, and compliance reporting. Using platforms like UiPath, Automation Anywhere, and Blue Prism, RPA developers help organisations save thousands of manual hours annually. India is the world's leading RPA talent hub — Gartner reports that over 60% of global RPA development work is executed by Indian professionals at service companies like TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, and Cognizant. There is strong global demand for Indian RPA specialists in BFSI, healthcare, and manufacturing sectors.
personality_traits: You are analytical and enjoy automating repetitive workflows
You are systematic and patient in identifying and mapping business processes
You are a problem-solver who enjoys finding elegant automation solutions
You are a strong communicator who can work with both technical teams and business stakeholders
You are a quick learner who keeps up with RPA platform updates and AI integration trends
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCM (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics) is preferred; Commerce with Mathematics is also acceptable for business process-focused roles.
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech CSE at NITs)
State engineering entrance exams (MHT-CET, KCET, COMEDK, AP EAMCET)
Company-specific technical assessments (coding rounds, logical reasoning tests)
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCM → B.Tech in CSE/IT or BCA/B.Sc. Computer Science (3-4 years) → Gain certification in UiPath Certified Advanced RPA Developer (UiPath Academy — free), Automation Anywhere Certified Advanced RPA Professional, or Blue Prism Developer Certification → Build a portfolio of 3-5 automation projects → Apply as an RPA Developer at IT service firms or directly with enterprises. UiPath Academy and Automation Anywhere University offer free courses making this one of the most accessible advanced tech certifications.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 50,000 – 2,00,000 per year (B.Tech government colleges); INR 1,00,000 – 4,00,000 per year (private colleges); UiPath Certified Developer exam: approximately INR 15,000–25,000; Automation Anywhere certification: INR 10,000–20,000
careers.career_growth_path: RPA Trainee → Junior RPA Developer → RPA Developer → Senior RPA Developer → RPA Solution Architect → RPA Practice Lead → Head of Intelligent Automation

work_environment.places_of_work: IT service companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL, Cognizant, Accenture), banking and financial services (HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, SBI Tech), insurance (LIC, Bajaj Allianz), manufacturing (Tata Steel, Maruti Suzuki), and global companies via remote roles.
work_environment.work_environment: Office or hybrid. Development work involves process mapping, coding bots, testing, and deploying in enterprise environments. Collaborative with business analysts and IT teams. Strong demand for overtime during project go-lives.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start an RPA consulting firm or build a vertical-specific automation platform (e.g., for healthcare or MSME accounting).

AICTE Pragati and Saksham Scholarship for differently-abled and girl students
UiPath Academic Alliance (free RPA platform access for students at registered colleges)

HDFC Credila and Avanse for engineering and computer science programmes

Under his leadership, the company has grown to a $6.8 billion valuation and trained over 3 million users globally, including hundreds of thousands of Indian RPA professionals through its free university programme.

careers.title: Perfusionist
careers.description: A Perfusionist is a highly specialised Allied Health professional who operates the heart-lung bypass machine (cardiopulmonary bypass circuit) during open-heart surgeries, enabling surgeons to operate on a still heart. They also manage extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO) in critically ill patients. In India, with over 60,000 open-heart surgeries performed annually at centres like AIIMS, Apollo, Fortis, Narayana Health, and Medanta, and with cardiac surgery growing at 12% CAGR, the demand for qualified perfusionists significantly outpaces supply. This is one of the highest-paying Allied Health careers in India. Indian perfusionists can also work in Gulf countries, UK, US (with ABCP certification), and Singapore.
personality_traits: You remain calm, focused, and precise under extreme pressure
You are technically meticulous — a small error can be life-threatening
You have strong spatial intelligence and mechanical aptitude
You are a team player who works seamlessly with surgeons, anaesthesiologists, and nurses
You are committed to lifelong learning as cardiac technology evolves rapidly
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCB (Physics, Chemistry, Biology) is mandatory.
entrance_exams: NEET-UG (required for admission to B.Sc. Perfusion Technology at many institutions)
State-level paramedical / allied health entrance exams (varies by state)
AIIMS Paramedical Entrance Exam (for AIIMS B.Sc. Perfusion Technology programmes)
Hospital-specific selection tests (Narayana Health, Apollo Hospitals training programmes)
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCB → B.Sc. in Perfusion Technology / Cardiovascular Perfusion (3-4 years) — available at AIIMS, Amrita, SRM, and Apollo Institute of Medical Sciences → Complete 2-year supervised clinical training (mandatory rotations: minimum 250 bypass procedures as primary perfusionist required for certification) → Obtain certification from the Indian Academy of Cardiovascular Perfusion (IACP) → Register with the National Commission for Allied and Healthcare Professions (NCAHP) → Apply at cardiac surgery centres. For international roles: ABCP (American Board of Cardiovascular Perfusion) certification for US; The College of Clinical Perfusion Scientists (CCPS) certification for UK.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 50,000 – 2,50,000 per year (government and government-aided institutions); INR 2,00,000 – 5,00,000 per year (private hospital-attached programmes); IACP certification: INR 5,000–10,000
careers.career_growth_path: Trainee Perfusionist → Clinical Perfusionist → Senior Perfusionist → Chief Perfusionist → Perfusion Programme Director / Academic and Research Perfusionist

work_environment.places_of_work: Cardiac surgery operating theatres at hospitals (AIIMS New Delhi, Apollo Hospitals, Narayana Health, Medanta, Fortis, Kokilaben Dhirubhai Ambani Hospital, Amrita Institute of Medical Sciences), ECMO-capable ICUs, and medical device companies (Getinge, LivaNova) for clinical support roles.
work_environment.work_environment: Highly specialised, high-stakes environment. Works during long cardiac surgeries (3-10 hours). On-call availability required. Demanding but extremely rewarding. Strong team culture in cardiac surgery units.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Limited direct entrepreneurship; however, can consult for hospital perfusion programme setup, provide training, or represent medical device companies.

scholarships: National Scholarship Portal: www.scholarships.gov.in (Allied Health Sciences scholarships)
Apollo Hospitals Education and Research Foundation (AHERF) scholarships
Narayana Health scholarships for paramedical students
State paramedical board scholarships (varies by state)
loans: Education loans from nationalised banks (allied health courses eligible)
HDFC Credila for healthcare professional programmes

AIIMS Bhopal, AIIMS Rishikesh – B.Sc. Allied Health Sciences (Perfusion Technology)

careers.example_from_field: Dr. Sujay Shad, Senior Director of Cardiac Surgery at Sir Ganga Ram Hospital, New Delhi, has performed over 12,000 open-heart surgeries and has publicly advocated for a minimum standard of 4 certified perfusionists per cardiac centre in India. His hospital's perfusion team manages both routine CABG procedures and complex ECMO cases — one of the busiest cardiac perfusion programmes in North India.

careers.title: Orthotist and Prosthetist
careers.description: An Orthotist designs and fits orthotic devices (braces, splints, supports) to support or correct musculoskeletal impairments. A Prosthetist designs and fits prosthetic limbs and devices to replace missing limbs. Together, they are Orthotists and Prosthetists (O&P), a critical Allied Health profession regulated in India by the Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI). With 2.7 crore persons with locomotor disability in India (Census data), a rapidly growing geriatric population, and increasing sports injury rehabilitation needs, there is a critical national shortage of trained O&P professionals. Indian O&P specialists can also work internationally — in UK, UAE, Singapore, Canada, and Australia, where Indian RCI qualifications are increasingly recognised.
personality_traits: You are compassionate and patient-centred — you help people regain mobility and independence
You are creative and technically skilled in design and fabrication
You are a strong communicator who builds trust with patients facing physical challenges
You are detail-oriented in measurements, fitting, and biomechanics assessment
You are comfortable with both clinical environments and workshop-based fabrication
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCB (Physics, Chemistry, Biology) is mandatory.
entrance_exams: NEET-UG (required for B.Sc./B.P&O admission at many government institutions)
State-level paramedical / allied health entrance exams
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCB → B.P.&O. (Bachelor of Prosthetics and Orthotics) — 4.5-year programme including 6-month internship, offered at NILD, NIMH, AIISH, and affiliated colleges → Register with the Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI) → Gain clinical experience (2-3 years) → Specialise via M.P.&O. (Master of Prosthetics and Orthotics) for advanced and academic roles. International recognition: ISPO (International Society for Prosthetics and Orthotics) membership enhances global career mobility.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 20,000 – 80,000 per year (government RCI-recognised institutions); INR 80,000 – 2,50,000 per year (private institutions); RCI registration fee: approximately INR 3,000–5,000
careers.career_growth_path: Intern Orthotist/Prosthetist → Orthotist/Prosthetist → Senior O&P Specialist → Clinical O&P Lead → Head of Rehabilitation Services / Academic Researcher

work_environment.places_of_work: Government hospitals (AIIMS, district hospitals under National Programme for Health Care of the Elderly), rehabilitation centres (Ali Yavar Jung National Institute, NIMH Secunderbad), disability NGOs (Jaipur Foot — BMVSS, HelpAge India), sports medicine clinics, orthopaedic hospitals (Manipal, Medanta), and self-run private clinics.
work_environment.work_environment: Clinical consultation, measurement and casting, workshop-based fabrication of devices, and patient gait training. Combines patient care with technical craftsmanship. Can be physically demanding. Rural outreach programmes involve travel.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can open a private orthotic-prosthetic clinic. Can also manufacture and supply low-cost devices — the Jaipur Foot (BMVSS) model demonstrates high social entrepreneurship potential.

scholarships: National Scholarship Portal: www.scholarships.gov.in (Disability sector scholarships)
RCI (Rehabilitation Council of India) merit-based fellowships
ADIP (Assistance to Disabled Persons for purchase of aids and appliances) scheme awareness scholarships (indirectly relevant)
ISPO (International Society for Prosthetics and Orthotics) Student Bursaries — open to Indian students
loans: SBI and PNB education loans for allied health science programmes

National Institute of Mental Health and Neuro Sciences (NIMHANS), Bengaluru

The Jaipur Foot has been fitted to over 1.3 million persons in 26 countries, and the BMVSS runs one of India's largest O&P training programmes — certifying hundreds of orthotists and prosthetists annually.

careers.title: Film Restoration Artist
careers.description: A Film Restoration Artist digitally preserves and restores damaged, deteriorated, or historically significant films using digital intermediate workflows, AI-powered noise reduction tools, and frame-by-frame retouching techniques. India holds the world's second-largest film archive — the National Film Archive of India (NFAI) in Pune has over 12,000 films, many in physical deterioration. Organisations like PVR Pictures, Shemaroo, and international studios partner with Indian restoration houses. International opportunities exist at major studios (Warner Bros., Universal), streaming platforms (Netflix, Amazon Prime — both of which fund Indian classic restorations), and UNESCO film heritage programmes.
personality_traits: You have a deep love for cinema and cultural heritage preservation
You are highly detail-oriented and patient with frame-by-frame retouching work
You have strong visual acuity and colour sensitivity
You are technically proficient with digital tools and enjoy learning new software
You are motivated by the cultural mission of saving cinematic history
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Arts/Humanities background is helpful but not mandatory. Science students with a passion for film can equally pursue this path.
entrance_exams: FTII Entrance Exam (Film and Television Institute of India, Pune) – for PG Diploma in Film Editing / Direction
SRFTI Entrance Exam (Satyajit Ray Film and Television Institute, Kolkata)
University entrance exams for BA/B.Sc. Film Studies or Media Studies
NID Entrance Exam (for digital media and communication design)
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 in any stream → Pursue BA in Film Studies, B.Sc. in Media Technology, or Diploma in Film/Video Editing (3-4 years) → Complete specialised training in digital film restoration workflows at FTII or international institutes such as L'Immagine Ritrovata (Bologna, Italy) or The Film Foundation → Gain proficiency in DaVinci Resolve, Adobe After Effects, Diamant Film Restoration Software, MTI Film, and AI restoration tools (NVIDIA Video4PC, DeOldify) → Apply to film archives (NFAI, regional film archives), post-production houses, or streaming platform restoration labs.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 30,000 – 1,50,000 per year (FTII, SRFTI government film schools); INR 1,00,000 – 4,00,000 per year (private media and film schools); specialised restoration workshops: INR 20,000 – 80,000
careers.career_growth_path: Restoration Trainee → Junior Restoration Artist → Film Restoration Specialist → Senior Restoration Supervisor → Head of Archival Restoration / Archive Director

work_environment.places_of_work: National Film Archive of India (NFAI, Pune), PVR Pictures restoration lab, Shemaroo Entertainment archive, regional state film archives (Kerala, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal), post-production houses, Netflix India (Classic Restoration programme), and overseas at studios or cultural film archives.
work_environment.work_environment: Desk-based, detail-intensive work using specialised software on high-resolution monitors. Often quiet, independent work environment. Film archive jobs carry a strong sense of cultural purpose. Restoration projects can take weeks to months per film.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start an independent film restoration studio serving production houses, state archives, and private collectors of classic films.

scholarships: FTII (Film and Television Institute of India) scholarships and fee concessions for meritorious and economically weaker students
Ministry of Information and Broadcasting (MIB) scholarships for film studies
UNESCO Memory of the World Programme fellowships (for archival/preservation professionals)
FIATIFTA (International Federation of Film Archives) Student Bursaries — open to Indian applicants
loans: HDFC Credila for film and media studies programmes

His work has been recognised internationally and has catalysed the creation of India's first formal film preservation policy discussions.

careers.title: Hydrologist
careers.description: A Hydrologist studies the distribution, movement, and quality of water in the Earth's surface, soil, and underground aquifers. They are essential for water resource management, flood prediction, drought preparedness, groundwater mapping, dam safety, and irrigation planning. India faces a critical water crisis — 54% of its land area is under moderate to high water stress (NITI Aayog Composite Water Management Index) — making hydrology one of the most nationally critical science disciplines. The Central Water Commission (CWC), National Water Development Agency (NWDA), state irrigation departments, and international organisations such as the World Bank, ADB, and UN-Water all hire Indian hydrologists.
personality_traits: You are passionate about water science and environmental conservation
You enjoy field work as much as data analysis
You are analytical and comfortable with hydrological modelling software
You are patient — hydrology involves long-term data collection and monitoring
You care about the environmental and social dimensions of water resource management
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCM (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics) or PCB with Geography; strong foundation in Mathematics is important.
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech in Civil Engineering — major gateway to hydrology careers)
GATE – Civil Engineering / Geology and Geophysics (for M.Tech Hydraulics or M.Sc. Hydrology)
UPSC Civil Services Examination (Indian Engineering Services / Forest Service — hydrology roles)
State Public Service Commissions (for state water department engineers)
IHE Delft / UNESCO-IHE entrance for international M.Sc. in Hydrology
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCM → B.Tech in Civil Engineering (4 years) with Water Resources electives OR B.Sc. in Earth Sciences/Geology/Environmental Science (3 years) → M.Tech in Hydraulics and Water Resources Engineering at IITs/NITs or M.Sc. in Hydrology at NIH (National Institute of Hydrology, Roorkee) — 2 years → Gain proficiency in HEC-HMS, HEC-RAS, SWAT, MODFLOW (groundwater), and GIS tools → Apply to CWC, NWDA, state water departments, or World Bank-funded water projects. National Institute of Hydrology (NIH), Roorkee, is India's premier research institution in this field.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 50,000 – 2,50,000 per year (government IITs/NITs); INR 30,000 – 80,000 per year (NIH Roorkee M.Sc.); INR 1,00,000 – 3,50,000 (private colleges)
careers.career_growth_path: Junior Hydrologist → Hydrologist → Senior Hydrologist → Superintending Engineer (Water Resources) → Chief Engineer / Director General (CWC / NWDA) → International Hydrology Consultant (World Bank, ADB, UN-Water)

work_environment.places_of_work: Central Water Commission (CWC), National Water Development Agency (NWDA), National Institute of Hydrology (NIH Roorkee), Central Ground Water Board (CGWB), state irrigation and water resources departments, international consultancies (DHI, Halcrow, AECOM), World Bank and ADB-funded projects, and research universities.
work_environment.work_environment: Mix of field surveys (river gauging, groundwater sampling), laboratory analysis, and computational modelling. Government roles involve significant field travel to rivers and reservoirs. International consulting can involve overseas postings.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a water resources consulting firm, a flood forecasting tech startup, or offer groundwater mapping services to municipalities and state governments.

scholarships: DST-INSPIRE Scholarship (for B.Sc./M.Sc. in Earth Sciences and Environmental Sciences)
CSIR-UGC JRF (Earth, Atmospheric, Ocean and Planetary Sciences)
Ministry of Jal Shakti research fellowships and project traineeships
loans: SBI Scholar Loan for IIT admissions
HDFC Credila and Avanse for postgraduate programmes

Amity University – M.Tech Water Resources Management
SRM Institute of Science and Technology – Civil Engineering
distance_learning: NPTEL online courses: Hydrology, Hydraulics, Groundwater Hydrology (IIT-based, free)
UNESCO-IHE Delft Open courseware (free foundational hydrology courses)
Coursera: Water Resources Management Specialisation (UC Davis — available to Indian students)

careers.example_from_field: Dr. R.D. Singh, former Director of the National Institute of Hydrology (NIH), Roorkee, led India's national water balance study which informed the National Water Policy 2012. His hydrological modelling research on Himalayan glacier melt and river basin management has been cited in over 200 international scientific publications.
careers.source: National Institute of Hydrology (NIH) Roorkee official website; NITI Aayog Composite Water Management Index (CWMI) 2019; Central Water Commission annual report 2023-24; Glassdoor India Hydrologist/Civil Engineer (Water) salaries (2025); Ministry of Jal Shakti programme documents

careers.title: Astrobiology Researcher
careers.description: An Astrobiology Researcher investigates the origin, evolution, and distribution of life in the universe — studying extreme life forms on Earth (extremophiles), conditions on Mars and icy moons like Europa and Enceladus, and biosignatures that could indicate extraterrestrial life. India's space programme under ISRO, particularly missions like Chandrayaan-3 (which discovered water ice signatures near the lunar south pole) and the planned Mangalyaan-3 and Venus Orbiter missions, is creating national relevance for astrobiological research. Indian students can pursue astrobiology through physics, chemistry, biology, and planetary science pathways, and access international roles at NASA, ESA, and major research universities globally.
personality_traits: You are profoundly curious about life in the universe and planetary science
You are comfortable with interdisciplinary research spanning chemistry, biology, and physics
You are patient and detail-oriented — astrobiology involves long research timelines
You are a strong scientific writer and communicator
You are globally mobile and open to international research collaborations
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCB or PCM (Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Mathematics); strong performance in both Biology and Chemistry or Physics is essential.
entrance_exams: JEE Advanced (for B.Tech Engineering Physics at IITs)
JAM (for M.Sc. Physics/Chemistry/Biological Sciences at IITs — gateway to astrobiology research)
JEST (Joint Entrance Screening Test — for PhD in Physics / Interdisciplinary Sciences)
CSIR-UGC NET – Life Sciences or Physical Sciences (for JRF fellowships)
NATA / ISRO Young Scientist Programme (for space science outreach)
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCB or PCM → B.Sc./B.Tech in Physics, Biology, Chemistry, or Earth Sciences (3-4 years) → M.Sc./M.Tech in Astrobiology, Planetary Science, or Biochemistry (2 years) — IIT Kharagpur, PRL Ahmedabad, and IISER campuses are leading centres → PhD in Astrobiology or Planetary Sciences (4-6 years, with PMRF or CSIR/DST fellowship) → Postdoctoral research at international labs (NASA Astrobiology Institute, ESA affiliated labs, MIT, Caltech). India has no dedicated undergraduate astrobiology programme yet — the field is accessed through interdisciplinary postgraduate and PhD routes.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 20,000 – 1,50,000 per year (IISERs, IITs — government funded); PMRF, CSIR-JRF, and INSPIRE fellowships cover PhD costs fully; minimal out-of-pocket for PhD students at premier institutions
careers.career_growth_path: Research Intern → Junior Research Fellow (JRF) → Senior Research Fellow (SRF) → Postdoctoral Researcher → Scientist / Assistant Professor → Senior Scientist / Associate Professor → Principal Investigator / Director of Astrobiology Research Programme

work_environment.places_of_work: Physical Research Laboratory (PRL), Ahmedabad (ISRO affiliated); IISERs (Pune, Mohali, Bhopal, Kolkata, Thiruvananthapuram); IITs; ISRO Space Science Programme; TIFR Mumbai; international: NASA Ames Research Center, ESA ESTEC, Caltech, MIT, ETH Zurich.
work_environment.work_environment: Research lab environment involving instrument development, sample analysis, computational modelling, and scientific writing. Collaborative internationally. Requires significant independent thinking and tolerance for uncertain timelines. Academic culture with publication expectations.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can found a planetary science data analytics company or astrobiology outreach/education startup.

scholarships: Prime Minister's Research Fellowship (PMRF) — up to ₹80,000/month for PhD at IITs/IISc/IISERs
CSIR-UGC JRF (Physical Sciences / Life Sciences) — ₹31,000/month + HRA
ISRO Research Fellowship for space science research
NASA-ISRO Astrobiology Exchange Fellowships (periodic joint programmes)
KVPY (Kishore Vaigyanik Protsahan Yojana) for undergraduate science students
loans: Education loans from nationalised banks (for postgraduate programmes before research fellowship)

government: Physical Research Laboratory (PRL), Ahmedabad, Gujarat (ISRO) — Planetary & Space Sciences, Astrobiology
Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER) — Pune, Mohali, Bhopal, Kolkata, Thiruvananthapuram
IIT Kharagpur – Centre for Astrobiology and Planetary Sciences (CAStPS)
Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR), Mumbai
Jawaharlal Nehru Centre for Advanced Scientific Research (JNCASR), Bengaluru
ISRO Space Science Programme (across multiple ISRO centres)
private: Amrita University, Coimbatore – interdisciplinary science programmes with space science modules
Manipal Academy of Higher Education – Physics/Chemistry research programmes
distance_learning: Coursera: Astrobiology and the Search for Extraterrestrial Life (University of Edinburgh — free audit, available to Indian students)
NASA Astrobiology Virtual Institute resources (free online learning)
NPTEL courses on Planetary Sciences and Geosciences (IIT-based, free)

careers.example_from_field: Prof. Pushkar Kopparla, an astrobiologist and planetary scientist at the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR), Mumbai, studies atmospheric chemistry on exoplanets and Venus using radiative transfer models. He is one of India's emerging voices in astrobiology and has collaborated with NASA and ESA researchers on Venus atmospheric biosignature detection.
careers.source: Physical Research Laboratory (PRL) Ahmedabad official website; IIT Kharagpur CAStPS research programme; ISRO Space Science Programme; NASA Astrobiology Institute (NAI) India collaborations; DST-INSPIRE Fellowship programme data 2024; IISER official websites

careers.title: Neuropsychologist
careers.description: A Neuropsychologist assesses and treats cognitive, behavioural, and emotional changes resulting from brain disorders, injuries, and neurodevelopmental conditions such as traumatic brain injury (TBI), stroke, dementia, ADHD, autism spectrum disorder (ASD), and epilepsy. They use standardised neuropsychological test batteries to understand how brain structure affects function, and design cognitive rehabilitation programmes. In India, with increasing awareness of brain disorders, the National Programme for Prevention and Control of Non-Communicable Diseases (NP-NCD), and NIMHANS' leadership in neuroscience, neuropsychology is a growing specialisation. Indian neuropsychologists are also eligible for international careers in the UK (BPS accreditation), USA (EPPP), Canada, and Australia.
personality_traits: You are intellectually curious about the relationship between the brain and behaviour
You are empathetic and skilled at working with patients with cognitive impairments
You are analytically strong and precise in psychometric test interpretation
You are patient — cognitive rehabilitation is a long, incremental process
You are committed to research alongside clinical practice
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Science with Biology and/or Psychology (where available) is advantageous.
entrance_exams: CUET UG / University entrance exams for BA/B.Sc. Psychology
CUET PG / University entrance for MA/M.Sc. Psychology with Clinical/Neuropsychology specialisation
NIMHANS PhD Entrance Examination (for PhD in Clinical Psychology/Neuropsychology)
RCI examination for clinical psychology registration
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 → BA/B.Sc. in Psychology (3 years) → MA/M.Sc. in Clinical Psychology (2 years, ideally at NIMHANS, AIIMS, or top universities) → M.Phil in Clinical Psychology (RCI mandated — 2 years, includes neuropsychological assessment training) → PhD in Neuropsychology (4-6 years, recommended for specialisation) → Register with RCI as a Clinical Psychologist → seek neuropsychology-specific supervised hours → Fellowship/Postdoc in Neuropsychology at NIMHANS or internationally. NIMHANS Bengaluru offers India's most prestigious neuropsychology training programme.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 20,000 – 80,000 per year (government universities — BA/MA/M.Phil); INR 80,000 – 2,50,000 per year (private); PhD at NIMHANS is fully funded via fellowship
careers.career_growth_path: Trainee Neuropsychologist → Neuropsychologist (Clinical) → Senior Neuropsychologist → Head of Neuropsychology Unit → Professor / Principal Investigator in Neuropsychology

work_environment.places_of_work: NIMHANS Bengaluru, AIIMS New Delhi, Apollo Hospitals, Fortis Healthcare, Medanta, government psychiatric hospitals, rehabilitation centres, neurology clinics, research universities, and international facilities.
work_environment.work_environment: Clinical assessment rooms, rehabilitation units, and research laboratories. Involves long individual test sessions (2-6 hours per patient). Emotionally demanding but deeply rewarding. Academic neuropsychologists split time between clinical work and research.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can establish a neuropsychological assessment and rehabilitation centre, or develop AI-assisted cognitive assessment tools.

scholarships: NIMHANS Bengaluru Research Fellowships for M.Phil and PhD students
ICMR (Indian Council of Medical Research) Short-Term Studentship and JRF
DST-INSPIRE Fellowship for interdisciplinary neuroscience PhD
loans: HDFC Credila for postgraduate psychology programmes

AIIMS New Delhi – M.Phil Clinical Psychology / Neuropsychology

University of Hyderabad – School of Medical Sciences (Psychology / Neuroscience)

careers.example_from_field: Prof. Bhaskara Rao, Head of Neuropsychology at NIMHANS Bengaluru, has authored standardised neuropsychological assessment batteries adapted for Indian populations — the first of their kind to account for Indian language diversity and educational heterogeneity. His tools are now used at over 50 hospitals across India for traumatic brain injury and dementia assessment.
careers.source: NIMHANS Bengaluru official programme brochure; RCI clinical psychology registration requirements; Glassdoor India Clinical Psychologist/Neuropsychologist salaries (2025); National Mental Health Survey of India 2015-16; ICMR Neuroscience Research Programme reports 2023

careers.title: Recreational Therapist
careers.description: A Recreational Therapist (also called Therapeutic Recreation Specialist) uses leisure activities — such as music, art, sports, drama, horticulture, and adapted outdoor activities — to improve the physical, emotional, cognitive, and social functioning of people with disabilities, mental health conditions, chronic illnesses, and ageing-related conditions. In India, this profession is growing within rehabilitation hospitals, psychiatric centres, special schools, aged care facilities, and wellness organisations. International opportunities are strong in the USA (CTRS certification), Canada, UK, and Australia, where Recreational Therapy is a recognised Allied Health profession. Indian graduates can access these markets through additional certification.
personality_traits: You are creative, energetic, and genuinely enjoy facilitating others' growth through play and activity
You are empathetic and skilled at adapting activities for diverse ability levels
You are patient, enthusiastic, and skilled at building therapeutic relationships
You are a problem-solver who designs activity-based interventions aligned with clinical goals
You are collaborative and comfortable working within interdisciplinary healthcare teams
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Arts/Humanities or Science with Biology are both suitable. Physical Education as a subject is advantageous.
entrance_exams: CUET UG / University entrance exams for BSc in Occupational Therapy or Physical Education
University entrance for BA/BSc in Psychology, Social Work, or Physiotherapy
NIMHANS / AIIMS entrance for postgraduate specialisation
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 → B.Sc. Occupational Therapy (which overlaps significantly with recreational therapy), B.Sc. in Physical Education, or BA in Psychology / Social Work (3-4 years) → Specialise via a PG Diploma or M.Sc. in Recreational Therapy / Therapeutic Recreation (available at select institutions and increasingly through international online programmes) → Obtain CTRS (Certified Therapeutic Recreation Specialist — USA-based global standard, accessible to Indian professionals via NCTRC) for international roles, or register with RCI for disability rehabilitation roles in India → Apply to rehabilitation hospitals, special schools, senior care centres, and mental health organisations.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 30,000 – 1,00,000 per year (government colleges — OT/PT/Psychology); INR 80,000 – 2,50,000 per year (private colleges); NCTRC CTRS exam (for international certification): approximately INR 15,000–30,000
careers.career_growth_path: Recreational Therapy Intern → Recreational Therapist → Senior Recreational Therapist → Programme Coordinator → Director of Therapeutic Services / Wellness Programme Director

work_environment.places_of_work: Rehabilitation hospitals (NIMHANS, CMC Vellore), psychiatric centres, special schools (for children with disabilities — RCI affiliated), aged care homes, cancer care centres, NGOs (HelpAge India, CBM India), wellness retreats, and corporate wellness departments.
work_environment.work_environment: Active, people-focused environment alternating between structured therapy rooms, outdoor spaces, and activity halls. Creative and emotionally rewarding. Group and individual session facilitation. Requires adaptability to each client's functional level.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a therapeutic wellness centre, a community recreation programme for disability inclusion, or an adaptive sports programme.

RCI merit fellowships for rehabilitation science students
Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment (disability sector scholarships)
CBM (Christian Blind Mission) India rehabilitation education scholarships
NCTRC Student Scholarship Programme (for CTRS preparation — open to international applicants)

Her programme has been cited in the Indian Journal of Occupational Therapy and has been adopted by three other hospitals in Kerala.

careers.title: Transportation Planner
careers.description: A Transportation Planner analyses, designs, and coordinates sustainable and efficient transportation systems — including metro rail, bus rapid transit, highways, cycling networks, and intermodal freight corridors. In India, with the National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP) committing ₹111 lakh crore in infrastructure investment, Smart Cities Mission developing 100 cities, and metro rail networks expanding to 50+ cities, transportation planning is a critically needed profession. Organisations such as NITI Aayog, DMRC, MMRC, RITES, and urban development authorities all hire transportation planners. Indian planners are also employed on World Bank, Asian Development Bank (ADB), and JICA-funded projects.
personality_traits: You are analytically strong and enjoy working with large-scale spatial and traffic data
You care about sustainable, equitable, and accessible urban mobility
You enjoy working at the intersection of engineering, policy, and social science
You are a strong communicator who can present plans to government and community stakeholders
You are a systems thinker who can integrate multiple transportation modes into a coherent network
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCM (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics); Mathematics is essential.
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech Civil Engineering — primary gateway)
GATE – Civil Engineering / Architecture and Planning (for M.Tech Urban / Transportation Planning)
CEED (for M.Des Urban Design/Planning at IITs)
UPSC Civil Services / IES (Indian Engineering Services) — Transport Engineering
State-level engineering entrance exams for Civil Engineering
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCM → B.Tech Civil Engineering (4 years) or B.Planning (Urban and Regional Planning — 4 years, available at NITs and School of Planning and Architecture) → M.Tech in Transportation Engineering or M.Plan in Urban and Regional Planning (2 years) → Gain proficiency in VISUM/PTV (transport modelling), ARCGIS for spatial analysis, VISSIM (microsimulation), and CUBE software → Apply to metro rail corporations, national highways planning bodies, RITES, Smart Cities Mission SPVs, or international consulting firms (AECOM, Jacobs, WSP India).
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 50,000 – 2,50,000 per year (government IITs, NITs, SPA); INR 1,50,000 – 5,00,000 per year (private colleges); VISUM/CUBE training courses: INR 15,000–40,000
careers.career_growth_path: Junior Transportation Planner → Transportation Planner → Senior Transportation Planner → Principal Planner → Director of Transportation Planning / Urban Mobility Commissioner

work_environment.places_of_work: Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC), Mumbai Metro Rail Corp (MMRC), Chennai Metro, RITES Ltd., National Highways Authority of India (NHAI), School of Planning and Architecture, Smart Cities Mission SPVs, international consulting (AECOM India, WSP India, Jacobs India), World Bank and ADB-funded projects.
work_environment.work_environment: Mix of office-based modelling and analysis with field surveys and stakeholder workshops. Collaborative with urban planners, traffic engineers, architects, and policymakers. Government projects have structured but slower timelines; consulting has faster-paced deliverables.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a transportation planning consulting firm specialising in non-motorised transport, transit-oriented development (TOD), or EV charging network planning.

scholarships: National Scholarship Portal: www.scholarships.gov.in (Engineering and Architecture scholarships)
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) fellowships for urban planning students
School of Planning and Architecture (SPA) merit scholarships
RITES Scholarship for Civil / Transportation Engineering students
World Bank Graduate Scholarship Programme (for postgraduate study in transport-related fields)
loans: SBI Scholar Loan for IIT admissions
HDFC Credila for M.Tech / M.Plan programmes

School of Planning and Architecture (SPA), Bhopal and Vijayawada

careers.example_from_field: E. Sreedharan, the 'Metro Man' of India, former Managing Director of Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC), transformed urban transportation planning in India. Under his leadership, DMRC built 280 km of metro network in Delhi — one of the largest and most efficient metro systems in Asia — and trained a generation of Indian transportation planners who now lead projects across 20 Indian cities.

careers.title: Urban Data Scientist
careers.description: An Urban Data Scientist applies data science, machine learning, and geospatial analytics to urban challenges — traffic congestion modelling, air quality prediction, housing affordability analysis, utility demand forecasting, and smart city governance. India's Smart Cities Mission (100 cities), the AMRUT 2.0 programme, and the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan are creating substantial demand for urban data scientists at municipal corporations, state urban development authorities, and Smart City SPVs (Special Purpose Vehicles). Indian urban data scientists can also work at think tanks (ORF, CPR, WRI India), global urban platforms (Bloomberg Cities, UN-Habitat), and consultancies (McKinsey Global Institute, Deloitte Urban).
personality_traits: You are analytically strong with a passion for cities and urban systems
You enjoy working with spatial, sensor, and administrative data
You are curious about the social dimensions of data — equity, access, representation
You are a strong visual communicator who can present data stories to policymakers
You are collaborative and thrive in interdisciplinary teams
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCM (Mathematics essential); Commerce with Mathematics is also a valid pathway via management/analytics routes.
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech CSE — data science foundations)
GATE – Computer Science / Statistics / Civil Engineering (for postgraduate specialisation)
CEED (for M.Des Urban Design/Planning at IITs)
CAT / GRE (for MBA in Urban Management / MPP with data analytics)
UPSC Civil Services (IAS — for policy-level urban planning roles)
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with Mathematics → B.Tech in CSE, B.Sc. in Statistics, or B.Planning/B.Tech Civil Engineering (3-4 years) → M.Tech/M.Sc. in Data Science, M.Plan with Urban Informatics specialisation, or MBA in Urban Management (2 years) → Gain proficiency in Python (geopandas, folium, shapely), R, SQL, Tableau, Power BI, Google Earth Engine, and urban simulation tools (UrbanSim, CityEngine) → Apply to Smart Cities SPVs, urban consulting firms, or research institutions. Indian students can also pursue international MPP/MPA programmes at Harvard Kennedy School, LSE, or Columbia SIPA with urban data specialisations.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 50,000 – 2,50,000 per year (government colleges/IITs); INR 1,50,000 – 5,00,000 per year (private); CEPT Ahmedabad M.Plan: INR 1,20,000 – 2,00,000 per year
careers.career_growth_path: Urban Analytics Analyst → Urban Data Scientist → Senior Urban Data Scientist → Lead Urban Insights Manager → Director of Urban Analytics / Chief Data Officer (Municipal Corporation)

work_environment.places_of_work: Smart City SPVs (Pune Smart City, GIFT City, Bengaluru Smart City), municipal corporations (BBMP, BMC, NDMC), national bodies (NITI Aayog, MoHUA), research organisations (WRI India, TERI, CPR), consulting firms (McKinsey, Deloitte, KPMG), and global organisations (UN-Habitat, World Bank).
work_environment.work_environment: Office and hybrid environment with data analysis, visualisation, and policy brief preparation. Regular stakeholder presentations to government officials. Collaborative with urban planners, engineers, and community organisations. Rewarding when insights directly inform public policy.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a civic technology startup, a GovTech analytics platform, or a city intelligence consulting firm.

CEPT University scholarships and merit-based fee waivers
Bloomberg Philanthropies Data for Health Fellowship (for urban health analytics — open to Indian professionals)

Her model was presented at the 2024 Smart City World Expo and adopted by Nagpur Smart City for adaptation.

careers.title: Agricultural Biotechnologist
careers.description: An Agricultural Biotechnologist applies the tools of molecular biology, genetic engineering, tissue culture, bioinformatics, and genomics to improve crop varieties, enhance agricultural productivity, develop biofuels, and create biopesticides. In India, with the approval of GM mustard (2022) and ongoing work on drought-resistant crops, Bt brinjal, and bio-fortified rice, agricultural biotechnology has become a national priority. The Department of Biotechnology (DBT), ICAR, and companies like Mahyco, Nuziveedu Seeds, Syngenta India, and Bayer CropScience hire agricultural biotechnologists. Indian students with biotech degrees can also access roles at Monsanto (Bayer), Syngenta, and Pioneer globally.
personality_traits: You are passionate about using science to solve food security and agricultural challenges
You enjoy laboratory work — genetic analysis, cell culture, molecular techniques
You are curious about the genetic basis of crop yield, disease resistance, and climate adaptation
You are analytical and rigorous in experimental design and data interpretation
You are aware of ethical dimensions around GMOs and biosafety regulations
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCB (Physics, Chemistry, Biology) is mandatory; Mathematics is an added advantage.
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech Biotechnology at NITs and reputed engineering colleges)
CUET UG / University entrance exams for B.Sc. Agriculture / B.Sc. Biotechnology
ICAR AIEEA-UG (for B.Sc. Agriculture and Allied Sciences at Agricultural Universities)
GATE – Biotechnology / Life Sciences (for M.Tech and PhD research fellowships)
CSIR-UGC NET – Life Sciences (for JRF fellowships in agricultural biotech research)
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCB → B.Sc./B.Tech in Biotechnology or B.Sc. Agriculture (3-4 years) → M.Sc./M.Tech in Agricultural Biotechnology, Plant Biotechnology, or Molecular Biology and Biochemistry (2 years) at ICAR institutions, IARI, or NBRI → PhD in Agricultural Biotechnology for research/academic roles (4-6 years, funded via DBT/CSIR/ICAR fellowships) → Apply at ICAR institutes, seed companies, agricultural biotech startups (Agrigenome, String Bio), or multinational agritech companies.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 20,000 – 1,50,000 per year (government agricultural universities/IITs); INR 80,000 – 3,00,000 per year (private institutions); PhD is largely funded via DBT/CSIR/ICAR fellowships
careers.career_growth_path: Research Associate → Scientist B → Scientist C → Senior Scientist → Principal Scientist → Director / Head of Crop Biotechnology Research

work_environment.places_of_work: ICAR institutes (IARI New Delhi, NBRI Lucknow, NRRI Cuttack), state agricultural universities (PAU Ludhiana, TNAU Coimbatore, ANGRAU Guntur), seed and agritech companies (Mahyco, Syngenta India, Bayer CropScience, UPL), and international research centres (IRRI Philippines, CIMMYT Mexico — accessible to Indian researchers).
work_environment.work_environment: Laboratory, greenhouse, and field trial environments. Combination of molecular lab work (PCR, CRISPR, tissue culture), bioinformatics analysis, and outdoor field trials. Seasonal fieldwork during planting and harvesting cycles. ICAR positions are government roles with strong job security.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start an agricultural biotech startup in areas such as CRISPR-edited seeds, biopesticides, microbial consortia for soil health, or plant tissue culture for export.

IARI (Indian Agricultural Research Institute) merit-based scholarships

His work has significant implications for India's edible oil imports and agricultural productivity.

careers.title: Textile Technology Engineer
careers.description: A Textile Technology Engineer applies engineering principles to design, develop, and optimise the processes and machinery used in the production of fibres, yarns, fabrics, and technical textiles. India is the world's second-largest textile exporter with the industry contributing approximately 2.3% of GDP and employing over 45 million people directly. The government's PLI (Production Linked Incentive) scheme for technical textiles and the ambitious goal of a $250 billion textile industry by 2030 are creating strong demand for qualified textile engineers in areas such as smart textiles, geotextiles, medical textiles (Meditech), and sustainable / recycled fibre technology. Indian textile engineers are globally competitive, with significant employment opportunities in the UK, UAE, Bangladesh, and Southeast Asia.
personality_traits: You are curious about material science and fibre/fabric properties
You enjoy applying engineering to manufacturing and process optimisation
You are interested in sustainable and innovative textile applications
You are detail-oriented in quality control and product testing
You enjoy working in both lab/testing and manufacturing floor environments
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCM (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics); Chemistry is particularly relevant.
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech Textile Technology at IITs, NITs, and DCT colleges)
State engineering entrance exams (MHT-CET, KCET, TANCET, AP EAMCET)
GATE – Textile Engineering and Fibre Science (for M.Tech and PhD admissions)
NIFT Entrance Exam (for B.Des and M.Des — design-oriented textile path)
University-specific exams at IIT Delhi, IIT Bombay, DKTE, PSG Tech
careers.educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 with PCM → B.Tech in Textile Technology / Textile Engineering (4 years) → M.Tech in Textile Engineering, Technical Textiles, or Fibre Science (2 years, recommended for specialisation) → Obtain certifications in CAD/CAM for textiles, Gerber Technology, and PLM software → Apply at textile mills, technical textile manufacturers, garment export firms, or research institutes. For smart textiles and medical textiles (Meditech), additional courses in electronics and material science are valuable.
careers.course_fee_inr: INR 50,000 – 2,00,000 per year (government IITs/NITs); INR 80,000 – 3,00,000 per year (private textile engineering colleges); GATE preparation courses: INR 10,000 – 30,000
careers.career_growth_path: Trainee Textile Engineer → Production Engineer → Quality Control Engineer → Production Manager → Plant Manager / Head of Textile R&D → Technical Director

work_environment.places_of_work: Textile mills (Arvind Mills, Raymond, Welspun, Trident Group), technical textile manufacturers (SRF Ltd., Garware Technical Fibres), export-oriented garment units, research institutes (ATIRA, MANTRA, BTRA), NIFT and NITRA, government textile testing labs.
work_environment.work_environment: Manufacturing floor and lab environment. Involves machinery supervision, quality testing, and process optimisation. Textile mills can be located in tier-2 and tier-3 cities (Tirupur, Surat, Ludhiana, Bhilwara). Field exposure is high, especially in production roles. R&D roles are more office and lab-based.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start a technical textiles manufacturing unit, a sustainable/recycled textiles startup, or a textile testing and certification lab.

scholarships: National Scholarship Portal: www.scholarships.gov.in (Engineering/Technical Textiles scholarships)
Ministry of Textiles scholarships (through NITRA, ATIRA, BTRA — textile research associations)
AICTE Pragati and Saksham Scholarship for girl students and differently-abled students
Textile Industry associations (IAF, ITMF) student bursaries
loans: HDFC Credila and Avanse for engineering programmes

Indian Institute of Technology Bombay (IIT Bombay) – Department of Fibre and Polymer Technology
Institute of Chemical Technology (ICT), Mumbai – Fibres and Textile Processing Technology
PSG College of Technology, Coimbatore – B.Tech Textile Technology (Aided)

Coimbatore Institute of Technology – Textile Technology
Vel Tech Rangarajan Dr. Sagunthala R&D Institute of Science and Technology, Chennai
